hotFiles.whyContended: Appears in ALL 4 backend-touching lenses. Every AI route lives here (chat/council/voice/vision/extract-log/generate-avatar/scene/comic/adventure/plan/story/hero-journey/analyze-behavior/handoff/digest/score-utterance/live-token). It is the single chokepoint for cost guards (image routes unmetered L743-807), consent enforcement (generate-avatar ignores the client consent flag), redaction wiring, safety screens, and child-profile-stringified-into-prompt leakage. Any avatar, cost, privacy, or i18n-content spec edits it.

Verified: aiQuota + coachMeter + requirePlusFeature middleware allow-lists are declared here (L116-124). The three image routes and /voice,/score-utterance,/generate-adventure,/generate-hero-journey are absent from every guard list. This is THE file where cost/entitlement enforcement for new AI surfaces must be added; contended by backend-ai and payment-model concerns.

Cited by 4 lenses (web-avatar, native, privacy, design-i18n-a11y). Consent is a client-only useState never sent to server (privacy); uses <input type=file> web API not @capacitor/camera, no capture affordance (native); bypasses PlayKit, no focus trap/Escape/return-focus, motion ignores reduced-motion (a11y); zero useLanguage, fully hardcoded EN (i18n). The flagship new surface that violates the most cross-cutting rules at once.

Verified: onCreated writes generated avatar dataUrl straight into photoUrl (L247) with avatarMeta set separately (L248). Source of the base64-in-Firestore bloat AND the photoUrl/avatar desync that silently downgrades hero/comic surfaces. Also the unconsented raw-face-photo upload path (onPickPhoto). Contended by web-avatar + privacy.

Hot in web-avatar, privacy, and growth lenses. photoUrl/avatar overload lives here; no consent/biometric/faceprint/voiceprint fields (COPPA blocker); the three competing 'score' concepts and bandSnapshot/devScore shapes are typed here. Any single-source-of-truth fix for child identity, consent ledger, or dev-score starts in this file.

Backend + native + privacy. Holds model ids (coach=claude-3-5-sonnet older snapshot, image=gemini-2.5-flash-image, live=gemini-2.0-flash-live-001), all tunable caps (maxOutputTokens 8192, AI_USER_HOURLY_LIMIT 80, FREE_COACH_MESSAGES_PER_DAY 10) but NO image-quota or daily-spend ceiling; bakes native CORS origins (capacitor://localhost, https://localhost) that only take effect after Cloud Run redeploy; entitlement-enforcement flag.

Provider abstraction (generateJson/Stream/streamText/generateImage) and route->model mapping. maxOutputTokens cap applied everywhere EXCEPT generateImage; image gen always forced to Gemini; no caching/circuit-breaker. Any model-currency, image-cost-guard, or prompt-caching spec lands here. Backend lens core file.

recordUsage/normalizeUsage emit ai.usage telemetry + daily Firestore rollup, but observe-only (no enforcement) and image-gen + child-ASR spend is invisible (image usageMetadata often null, ASR never records). The file to extend for token/spend-based budgeting that the founder dashboard and payment model need.

Design-system primitive set with baked-in a11y defaults. Renders strings verbatim (no i18n awareness), Celebrate star aria-label hardcoded English, no RTL handling (speech-bubble corners physically left-anchored), color-only ChoiceTile state. The two flagship new surfaces (AvatarCreator, MimicMatch) bypass it entirely. Central to design+i18n+a11y fixes.

Hebrew coverage is a facade: only prac.<tab>.title/.sub exist in HE (feed PlayHeader only); 100% of in-game strings, AvatarCreator, MimicMatch, RewardsCard are hardcoded EN. Israel-first GTM requires mass string extraction here PLUS a content-data localization seam that doesn't exist.

Backend + privacy. Pluggable whisper/soapbox seam, default 'none'->409. SoapBox mapping is scaffolding only (ADAPT-TO-VENDOR placeholder). The comment says 'gate behind parental consent at the app layer' but that gate was never built -> child voiceprint leaves to a third party with no consent record, no retry, no usage telemetry. Highest COPPA exposure.

Growth-loop engine: domainBands, weeklyMissionPlan, speechDose, developmentTrajectory, developmentScore all live here. Two of the three overlapping 'score' concepts originate in this file; any growth/closed-loop/longitudinal work risks editing the wrong score or diverging numbers across tabs.

Native shell bootstrap (status bar/keyboard/splash) is solid but covers ONLY cosmetics. It is the natural home for the missing capability/permission bootstrap and for @capacitor/local-notifications scheduling that the JITAI/rhythm closed-loop needs. Contended by native + growth lenses.

Has NO NSCameraUsageDescription / NSMicrophoneUsageDescription / notification strings. MimicStudio camera, SpeechCoach mic, Gemini Live audio all use web getUserMedia in WKWebView -> denied/broken on iOS native as-is. Must be edited before any camera/mic/AI feature ships on iOS.
Declares ONLY INTERNET. Missing CAMERA, RECORD_AUDIO, POST_NOTIFICATIONS -> MimicStudio, SpeechCoach, reminders broken on Android native. Sibling to Info.plist for the native permission gap.

surfaceStates: Avatar Creator (descriptor + photo -> AI character): REAL on web. Stores generated avatar as base64 data: URL in childProfile.photoUrl (ProfileEditDrawer:247), no Storage/CDN offload, no client/server rate-limit, consent is client-only and unenforced server-side. Web-only.
Avatar rendering (Avatar.tsx, used in ProfileSwitcher/Sidebar/Coach): REAL. Plain , no onError->initials fallback, no size handling for giant data: URLs. Web.

Child-as-hero (HeroAvatar/useHeroAvatar): REAL but fragile. Branches on photoUrl.startsWith('data:') && avatar; a Google/uploaded remote photo silently downgrades to seeded illustration; isGenerated and photoUrl can desync. Web.

Hero Journey story player + scene/comic art (HeroJourneyTab/HeroScenePlayer): REAL. Uses generateComic per beat; in-memory cache only -> regenerates every panel on reload; panels never persisted to HeroJourneyRun.render; generateScene endpoint exists but is dead-ish. Web. High image cost.
Hero Comics (Academy): REAL. Comic is local state only, never saved (download-only); without a hero avatar the protagonist is generic. Web.

Emotion-state avatar (EmotionAvatar in FeelingsLab): REAL but thin. Composition over Avatar + emoji/aura, no per-emotion generation; only 6 emotions mapped; shows raw photo not the stylized hero -> identity inconsistency. Web.
Rewards/cosmetics (RewardsCard): REAL but disconnected. Text/emoji badges, no avatar accessories/skins, stats recomputed each render, no persisted unlock record. Web. No i18n.

AI Coach text (CoachTab/CoachAnswerCards): REAL. Uses ArborMascot not the child hero as the AI face; brittle regex risk fallback; teacher-handoff button only switches tab + toasts (doesn't inject note). Web.

AI Coach voice (Gemini Live HD + browser STT/TTS): REAL on web. HD gated only on server API key presence, NOT user entitlement; browser TTS robotic, no Hebrew voice, no barge-in, no avatar/visual feedback. /live/token session bypasses all server metering. Web; broken on native (mic perms absent).
Read-aloud/TTS (shared lib/tts.ts): THIN. Global speechSynthesis.cancel() means starting in one surface stops another; fixed rate/voice, no per-surface isolation. Cross-cutting web.

Model router / provider abstraction: REAL. Clean ModelProvider interface (GeminiDev / VertexGemini / ClaudeVertex), 4 logical routes, multimodal fallback, maxOutputTokens cap on text (NOT image), withModelRetry 3x. No caching, no circuit breaker. Backend.

Token-usage telemetry (recordUsage/usageRollup): REAL but observe-only. No budget enforcement; image gen logs route hardcoded creative_low_risk and often null token counts; aborted streams lose partial usage. Backend.

AI routes (chat/council/voice/vision/image/plan/story/hero/etc): REAL + wired with redaction + escalation screen. /council fans out N parallel scholar calls + synthesizer = most expensive request. Backend.

Cost/quota/entitlement guardrails: THIN. aiQuota (80/hr request-count) covers only chat/council/vision/generate-plan/generate-story/analyze-behavior/generate-handoff/digest (verified createApp:116-119). Image routes + /voice + /score-utterance + /generate-adventure + /generate-hero-journey are UNMETERED. Quota is request-count not token/spend. Entitlements default unenforced outside prod. Backend.

Child ASR seam (score-utterance): THIN. whisper fallback works; soapbox is scaffolding only (placeholder mapping, ADAPT-TO-VENDOR); no retry, no usage telemetry, cost-unbounded; no consent gate. Backend/cross-cutting.

Capacitor shell / plugin set: THIN. Only 5 cosmetic plugins; NO camera/push/local-notifications/filesystem. Bundle id still placeholder app.arbor.family. iOS never built (needs Mac). Cross-cutting native.

api-base fetch shim (runtime.ts): REAL/works on native. Rewrites relative /api/* to API_BASE (prod web URL) before React mounts; native CORS baked in env.ts but only live after Cloud Run redeploy. Cross-cutting.
Native shell bootstrap (status bar/keyboard/splash): REAL/solid. Gated to native, dynamic imports no-op on web. Cosmetic only; no capability/permission bootstrap. Cross-cutting.
Domain bands + Development Copilot: REAL closed-loop within a session (practice->bands->recommend->next mission); read-only guidance, no server persistence of the recommendation. Cross-cutting.

Weekly mission plan: REAL closed-loop, auto-regenerates per ISO week from this child's unreached milestones reweighted by last-7-day practice; NOT persisted as a weekly artifact (no plan-vs-done record). Cross-cutting.
Speech dosage (ASHA): REAL on measurement only; reports trials/sessions but does not auto-adjust targets or nudge when behind. Cross-cutting.

Longitudinal trajectory + weekly band snapshots: REAL closed-loop on capture (one bandSnapshot/ISO-week to Firestore via useCopilot) but mount-driven side effect -> no background job; rendered only inside Copilot, not promoted to dashboard. Cross-cutting.
Longitudinal Development Score (growth/devScore.ts): THIN. Persists prior snapshot to localStorage ONLY -> resets on device switch / native; a SECOND parallel score system to signals.ts. Web.

Rhythm / JITAI predictive timing: THIN + OPEN LOOP. predictRhythm is a real tested engine but purely a passive in-app strip; NO notifications/scheduled nudges; no notification infra exists; predictions never persisted. Cross-cutting.

Growth-plan loop (lib/plans.ts/PlansTab): THIN + OPEN LOOP. planProgress/suggestedChallenges real but generation fully manual; on completion UI tells user to start a fresh plan; no auto-regen, no linkage into mission/band loop. Cross-cutting.
PlayKit primitive set: REAL but i18n-blind, English-hardcoded star aria-labels, no RTL geometry, color-only ChoiceTile state. Cross-cutting.

MimicStudio camera (MediaPipe FaceLandmarker): REAL on web (best-in-class privacy, on-device, frames never leave); MISSING on native (getUserMedia needs Info.plist/Manifest perms that are absent) -> broken iOS+Android. MediaPipe WASM/model from CDN (network-dependent).
SpeechCoach mic + Gemini Live audio: REAL on web; MISSING on native (no NSMicrophoneUsageDescription / RECORD_AUDIO; Web Speech API unsupported in WKWebView). Broken native.
Notifications / reminders (RemindersCard): THIN. Web Notification API only (foreground/in-app), no push/local-notifications; silent no-op on iOS WKWebView, flaky Android. Cross-cutting.

Consent model / data retention / COPPA-2026 readiness (system-wide): MISSING. No consent data model, no VPC flow, no consent ledger, no AI-training consent, no retention TTL/deletion lifecycle, no biometric classification. Cross-cutting.

PII redaction before model calls: REAL but text-only and partial (name/email/phone only; address/DOB/school/siblings NOT redacted; childProfile JSON.stringified raw into vision/coach prompts; no audio/image redaction). Backend.

Safety screening: REAL on text. Trilingual EN/HE/NL input escalation wired widely; output lexical screen always-on, semantic classifier OFF by default and fails OPEN; NO safety screen on generated IMAGES. Backend.
platformGaps: Camera is web getUserMedia (MimicStudio/MimicMatch via MediaPipe), NOT @capacitor/camera. iOS Info.plist has no NSCameraUsageDescription and Android Manifest declares only INTERNET (no CAMERA) -> camera DENIED/broken on both native platforms today.

Microphone is web getUserMedia (geminiLiveClient.ts) + Web Speech API (speech.ts), NOT native. iOS lacks NSMicrophoneUsageDescription, Android lacks RECORD_AUDIO -> SpeechCoach + Gemini Live audio broken on native. Web Speech SpeechRecognition is additionally unsupported inside iOS WKWebView / unreliable in Android WebView even with perms.

Notifications/reminders use the web Notification API (RemindersCard), which is a no-op in iOS WKWebView and flaky in Android WebView. No @capacitor/push-notifications or @capacitor/local-notifications; no APNs/FCM config; no POST_NOTIFICATIONS (Android 13+) or iOS notification strings -> no real/background/scheduled reminders on native.

JITAI rhythm (predictRhythm) is web-only passive UI with NO delivery mechanism on any platform; closing the loop requires native @capacitor/local-notifications scheduling + permission flow in native.lib that does not exist.

Avatar photo picking uses <input type=file accept=image/*> (no capture attr) -> on native opens OS file picker (works without camera perm) but there is no 'take a photo' affordance; a future @capacitor/camera switch would then need NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription.

Development Score trend persists to localStorage only -> silently resets on the Capacitor native apps and every new device; the longitudinal value prop is broken outside one browser. Needs migration to a per-child Firestore collection like bandSnapshots.
MediaPipe WASM+model and all AI/avatar API calls require live network from the webview; no offline/bundled fallback, so any CDN or CORS issue kills the avatar+games loop on native.
Backend CORS for native origins (capacitor://localhost, https://localhost) is baked into env.ts but only takes effect after a Cloud Run redeploy -> native AI/avatar calls blocked until that ships.

Bundle id is still placeholder app.arbor.family (cannot change after first store listing) and iOS has never been built/archived (needs a Mac). A stale dist/ is committed under ios/App/App/public -> risk of shipping an old frontend if npx cap sync is skipped.
oklab color-mix in the .arbor-play kids-zone wash has no fallback -> degrades on older Safari / Capacitor iOS WebView.
Native shell only covers cosmetic status-bar/keyboard/splash; there is no capability/permission bootstrap, which is the unifying gap behind every camera/mic/notification feature above.
crossCuttingConstraints: COPPA-2026 / privacy (HARD GAP, highest exposure): No consent data model, ledger, or VPC flow exists anywhere (grep 'consent' = one ephemeral AvatarCreator checkbox + comments). Child VOICEPRINT (SpeechCoach audio) is uploaded to a third-party ASR vendor with ZERO consent gate; child FACEPRINT travels via avatar/vision with an unenforced client-only checkbox; raw face photos persist to Storage with no TTL/deletion. Requires: faceprint+voiceprint classified as PII, separate AI-training consent, auditable persisted consent records, retention/deletion lifecycle, and encoded vendor no-train/DPA/region controls (today only asserted in UI copy/comments).

AI cost: Image routes (generate-avatar/scene/comic) are the highest unit-cost calls and are UNMETERED — no aiQuota, no entitlement gate, no per-user image cap, only a 30 req/min/IP global limit (verified absent from createApp.ts:116-119). /voice and /score-utterance also unbounded. Quota is request-count not token/spend (one /council = N parallel calls counts as 1). Telemetry exists but enforces nothing; image + ASR spend is invisible in the rollup. Generated base64 avatars/comics live in Firestore/state with no CDN offload and are re-sent as references and regenerated on every render -> multiplied cost.

Design-system / PlayKit: Adopted by most practice tabs but the two flagship NEW AI surfaces (AvatarCreator, MimicMatch — both child-facing) bypass it, missing its touch-target and a11y defaults and drifting visually. PlayKit itself is i18n-blind and RTL-unaware (speech-bubble corners left-anchored; English-hardcoded star aria-labels). Confetti colors hardcoded instead of reading tokens.

i18n Hebrew (Israel-first GTM blocker): Only prac.<tab>.title/.sub are translated (feed PlayHeader). 100% of in-game body strings, AvatarCreator, MimicMatch, RewardsCard are hardcoded English. Deeper structural blocker: practice CONTENT (content.ts IPA/'S as in Sun', faceMatch.ts, cosmetics.ts) is English-phonetics-specific with no localization seam — Hebrew needs a content-data architecture change, not just string extraction. RTL untreated in .arbor-play.

Accessibility (WCAG): Reduced-motion only half-honored — global CSS guard neutralizes CSS animations but NOT motion/react (Framer) JS transitions driving PlayShell, AvatarCreator modal, AdventuresTab (only celebrateBurst checks the preference). AvatarCreator dialog lacks focus trap / Escape / return-focus / initial focus. Color-only state on ChoiceTile and MimicMatch meter. Win/score moments (MimicMatch meter, 'You did it!') lack role=progressbar/aria-live -> invisible to screen readers. Style/mode toggles convey selection by color only (no aria-pressed/role=radio).

Safety: Generated IMAGES (avatar/scene/comic) bypass the output safety screen entirely (text-only); semantic output classifier is OFF by default and fails OPEN, so prod relies on a regex lexical floor; input escalation is regex-only (misses paraphrase/obfuscation).

Single-source-of-truth fragmentation: (a) child identity has THREE faces — stylized hero, EmotionAvatar raw photo, ArborMascot coach persona; photoUrl is overloaded by both upload and generated avatar with avatar metadata set separately (desync risk). (b) THREE overlapping development-score systems (signals.developmentScore practice-consistency; signals.developmentTrajectory band-history Firestore; growth/devScore milestone-share localStorage) with diverging numbers and persistence. (c) Growth loops (plans/missions/rhythm) are independent silos that don't feed each other — 'closed-loop growth' is true only within each silo. Snapshot/score writes are mount-driven side effects with no background job, so families who never open the view accumulate no history.

specs.title: Avatar native capture (Capacitor Camera) iOS/Android + web parity

spec.title: Avatar native capture (Capacitor Camera) iOS/Android + web parity
spec.goal: Replace the web-only `<input type="file">` photo path in AvatarCreator with a platform-aware capture flow that uses @capacitor/camera (take photo OR pick from library) on iOS/Android while keeping the exact web getUserMedia/file-input behavior on web. Wire iOS Info.plist + Android Manifest permission strings so capture is not denied. Preserve the privacy contract: the reference photo is downscaled on-device, sent once to /api/generate-avatar, and never stored (only the generated stylized dataUrl + avatar metadata persist). No backend prompt/route logic changes required.
filesToCreate: C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\lib\capture.ts — new platform-aware capture helper: `capturePhoto(opts): Promise<{dataUrl:string}|undefined>`. On native (isNativePlatform) dynamic-import @capacitor/camera (`Camera.getPhoto({ source: CameraSource.Prompt | Camera | Photos, resultType: CameraResultType.DataUrl, quality: 70, width: 512, allowEditing: false, correctOrientation: true })`) and normalize the returned dataUrl through fileToThumbnail-equivalent downscale; on web fall back to a programmatic <input type=file accept=image/* capture=...> -> fileToThumbnail(file,512,0.85). Mirror the dynamic-import / no-op-on-web pattern from src/lib/native.ts so the web bundle never pulls native plugin code.

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\lib\__tests__\capture.test.ts — unit tests: web path calls fileToThumbnail and returns a data: URL; native path mocks @capacitor/core isNativePlatform=true + mocked @capacitor/camera Camera.getPhoto and asserts opts mapping (DataUrl/quality/source) and graceful undefined on user-cancel.
filesToEdit: C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\profile\AvatarCreator.tsx — replace the `<input type=file accept=image/* onChange={onPickPhoto}>` (L176) with a native-aware affordance. On native render two buttons (Take a photo / Choose from library) that call the new capture.ts helper with CameraSource.Camera / Photos respectively; on web keep current file-input behavior. Route both into existing onPickPhoto(file|dataUrl) -> setRefPhoto. Keep consent gating: capture buttons disabled until `consent` is checked (same rule as today, L168/L176). No change to generate()/api.generateAvatar payload shape ({ photo: { dataUrl } }).

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\profile\ProfileEditDrawer.tsx — OPTIONAL parity: the 'Upload a photo instead' file input (L162) also gains a native capture affordance via capture.ts so raw-photo upload works on native too; onPickPhoto already accepts a File but must be widened to accept a dataUrl on native. Keep avatarMeta=undefined on raw upload (L46). Touch minimally — this is a hot file (base64-in-Firestore + photoUrl/avatar desync live here); do NOT refactor the photoUrl storage path in this mission.

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\ios\App\App\Info.plist — add NSCameraUsageDescription and NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription (user-facing copy explaining the photo is used once to make a cartoon and never stored). No mic key needed for this mission.

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\android\app\src\main\AndroidManifest.xml — add <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.CAMERA"/> and (Android 13-) READ_MEDIA_IMAGES / READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE for library pick. FileProvider authority `${applicationId}.fileprovider` already declared (L23-29), which @capacitor/camera reuses — verify file_paths xml covers cache.
C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\package.json — add dependency "@capacitor/camera": "^8.x" (align major with installed @capacitor/core ^8.4.0).

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\android\app\src\main\res\xml\file_paths.xml — verify/add a cache-path entry so the camera plugin can write the temp capture (Capacitor Camera writes to cache). Create the entry if missing.
apiChanges: NONE. /api/generate-avatar (src/routes/api.ts L743) already accepts { photo: { dataUrl }, descriptors, style }, validates image mime + 6MB cap (L759-773), safety-screens free text, and never stores the reference. api.generateAvatar in src/lib/api.ts (L128) is unchanged. This mission is intentionally client/native-only and does NOT touch the contended src/routes/api.ts or src/server/createApp.ts.
dataModel: NONE. ChildProfile.photoUrl + ChildProfile.avatar { style, source: 'descriptor'|'photo', createdAt } in src/types.ts (L12-18) already capture what onCreated writes. The known base64-in-photoUrl bloat + photoUrl/avatar desync are explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this mission (separate backlog item).
dependencies: npm: @capacitor/camera (^8.x, match @capacitor/core ^8.4.0) added to app/package.json
After install: `npx cap sync ios && npx cap sync android` to register the plugin in the native projects (regenerates Pods / Gradle plugin wiring)

Backend CORS: native origins capacitor://localhost + https://localhost are already in env.ts default allowlist (L95-96). Confirm the deployed Cloud Run revision was built after these were added; if not, a redeploy via cloudbuild.prod.yaml is required for native /api/generate-avatar to succeed (CORS_ORIGINS). No env var change needed beyond ensuring current revision includes them.
iOS build requires a Mac + Xcode (iOS shell has never been archived); Android can be built on the current machine.
Bundle id is still placeholder app.arbor.family (capacitor.config.ts L18) — must be set before first store listing but is not a blocker for dev/sideload testing of this feature.
steps: 1. In worktree, add @capacitor/camera to app/package.json and `npm install`.

2. Create src/lib/capture.ts with capturePhoto() — native branch dynamic-imports @capacitor/camera (DataUrl, quality 70, width 512, correctOrientation), web branch uses programmatic file input + fileToThumbnail; both return a downscaled data: URL or undefined on cancel. Reuse runtime.ts isNativePlatform.

3. Edit AvatarCreator.tsx: replace the photo file <input> (L176) with isNativePlatform-conditional Take-photo / Choose-library buttons calling capturePhoto({source}); keep consent gating and setRefPhoto(...). Leave generate()/payload untouched.

4. Edit ProfileEditDrawer.tsx 'Upload a photo instead' (L160-163) to also offer native capture via capture.ts; widen onPickPhoto to accept dataUrl; keep avatarMeta=undefined for raw uploads. Minimal touch.
5. Add NSCameraUsageDescription + NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription to ios/App/App/Info.plist.
6. Add CAMERA + READ_MEDIA_IMAGES/READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE to AndroidManifest.xml; verify res/xml/file_paths.xml has a cache-path entry.
7. Run `npx cap sync ios && npx cap sync android`. IMPORTANT: a stale dist/ is committed under ios/App/App/public — run `npm run build` BEFORE cap sync so the native bundle ships current frontend.
8. Write capture.test.ts; run `npm run lint` (tsc) and `npm test`.
9. Build Android (Android Studio / gradle) and verify capture+library on a device/emulator; confirm /api/generate-avatar returns over the network from native origin.
10. iOS: on a Mac, `npx cap open ios`, set bundle id + signing, archive, verify camera permission prompt uses the new Info.plist string and capture works.
testPlan: Unit (vitest, scripts/vitest.config.mjs): capture.test.ts — web path returns data: URL via mocked fileToThumbnail; native path maps source Camera/Photos to CameraSource and returns the plugin dataUrl; user-cancel -> undefined (no throw).
Lint: `npm run lint` (tsc --noEmit) passes with the new dep types.
Existing suite: `npm test` stays green (241+ tests) — AvatarCreator change must not break existing render tests.
Web manual: AvatarCreator photo mode still opens the OS file picker, consent still gates, generated avatar still returns and renders (Avatar.tsx).

Android device manual: Take-a-photo opens camera after granting permission; Choose-from-library opens gallery; both produce a downscaled dataUrl; /api/generate-avatar succeeds (no CORS error in webview console).
iOS device manual: first capture shows the new NSCameraUsageDescription prompt; deny path surfaces the existing in-modal error, not a crash; allow path generates an avatar.
Privacy assertion: confirm no raw photo is persisted — only result dataUrl + avatar meta written by ProfileEditDrawer onCreated (L246-248); inspect Firestore write payload.
risks: Stale committed dist/ under ios/App/App/public can ship an old frontend if cap sync runs without a fresh `npm run build` — explicitly build first (step 7).

Capacitor Camera returns large DataUrls; if downscale is skipped the base64 inflates the request and the existing 6MB server cap (api.ts L768) can 413 — enforce width/quality + fileToThumbnail in capture.ts.
Android 13+ media permission split: READ_MEDIA_IMAGES vs legacy READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE; wrong choice silently fails the library pick on some API levels.
CORS: if the live Cloud Run revision predates the native origins in env.ts (L95-96), native /api/generate-avatar will be blocked until redeploy — verify before QA.
iOS cannot be built/verified without a Mac; treat iOS as a separate verification leg.
Widening ProfileEditDrawer onPickPhoto signature risks touching the hot photoUrl path — keep the diff surgical; do not change the data: URL storage behavior in this mission.
Plugin major-version mismatch (@capacitor/camera vs core v8) breaks the native build — pin to ^8.x.
spec.deployNotes: "Frontend + native shells change; backend code unchanged. Web: ships with the normal Firebase Hosting frontend build (no behavior change on web, file input preserved). Native: requires `npm run build` then `npx cap sync` and a fresh Android build / iOS archive. Ensure the deployed backend revision includes the native CORS origins (env.ts L95-96); redeploy via cloudbuild.prod.yaml only if the current revision predates them. No new env vars, no Firestore/schema migration. Verify on each platform: web file-picker unchanged; Android camera + library; iOS camera prompt copy + capture (Mac-only). Bundle id app.arbor.family should be finalized before any store submission."

missingPieces: Test infra: vitest runs environment:'node' (scripts/vitest.config.mjs L5); NO jsdom/happy-dom/@testing-library installed. capture.test.ts web path exercises fileToThumbnail -> FileReader/Image/<canvas>, which do not exist under node. The unit test plan is not runnable without adding a DOM environment + canvas shim (new devDeps + per-file // @vitest-environment jsdom or config change). Spec does not specify this.

Native-path test mocking is unspecified and friction-laden: runtime.ts caches `export const isNativePlatform = Capacitor.isNativePlatform()` at module load (L17). 'Reuse runtime.ts isNativePlatform' means the value can't be flipped per test. The native test needs vi.mock('@capacitor/core') before import AND capture.ts must read platform dynamically. No existing test in the repo mocks @capacitor/core; no precedent to copy.

The spec omits the server consent gate: /api/generate-avatar is wrapped by gateFacePhoto = requireConsent(consentStore,'face_processing', { appliesWhen: req.body.photo||req.body.avatar }) (api.ts L83-85, L854). Generation with a photo requires a RECORDED face_processing consent, not just the in-modal checkbox. Native QA following the testPlan will hit a 403 with no explanation. Must be called out (and the native capture buttons should ensure the consent record is written, matching web).

ProfileEditDrawer onPickPhoto cannot just be 'widened to accept a dataUrl': current impl (L36-52) calls fileToThumbnail(file,256,0.85) then uploadChildPhoto(user.uid, id, thumb), both requiring a File. A native dataUrl needs a distinct branch (skip File thumbnailing; decide whether to still uploadChildPhoto the dataUrl). This is real logic, not a signature widen / 'minimal touch'.

capture.ts contract underspecified: returns {dataUrl} on native but plan says web returns a 'data: URL' string while AvatarCreator's onPickPhoto(file?) takes a File. The web branch must route the produced dataUrl into setRefPhoto directly (not via onPickPhoto(file)), or onPickPhoto must be refactored. The two return shapes ({dataUrl:string}|undefined vs File) are not reconciled.
conflicts: package.json: adding @capacitor/camera is a shared-file edit that will merge-conflict with any sibling native-plugin mission also editing dependencies; coordinate a single dependency-bump owner or expect a trivial-but-required manual merge.

AvatarCreator.tsx and ProfileEditDrawer.tsx are touched by avatar/games/profile sibling missions; concurrent edits to the photo-mode block (AvatarCreator L179-194) and the avatar affordance (ProfileEditDrawer L148-169) risk clobber. Sequence M1 before/after those rather than in parallel.

npx cap sync regenerates ios/android native project files (Podfile, capacitor.build.gradle, plugin registration); any sibling mission that also runs cap sync will produce overlapping generated-file diffs.
Stale committed dist/ under ios/App/App/public is shared state: if two native missions cap sync without coordinating a fresh npm run build, they ship divergent bundles.
safetyGaps: Server-side face_processing consent gate (api.ts L83-85) is unaddressed in the UX: the in-modal checkbox is UI-only and does NOT write the consentStore record the backend enforces. Privacy-correct, but native users who consented in-modal can still be blocked (403) unless the flow records face_processing consent. Must be wired or documented.

iOS NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription: spec adds it, but if 'take photo' uses CameraSource.Camera only and library is offered, also confirm NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription is not needed (it isn't for read-only pick) — acceptable, but verify Apple review copy explicitly states 'used once, never stored, never used to train'.

Android 13+ media permission split (READ_MEDIA_IMAGES) vs <=12 (READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE with maxSdkVersion) must be expressed correctly in the manifest or library pick silently fails on a subset of devices — spec flags the risk but does not give the exact manifest lines.

a11y: new native 'Take a photo' / 'Choose from library' buttons must carry accessible labels and preserve the consent-disabled state (aria-disabled), matching the existing label/input pattern; not specified.
corrections: Add jsdom (or happy-dom) as a devDependency and either set test.environment to 'jsdom' for DOM-touching specs or add `// @vitest-environment jsdom` to capture.test.ts; mock canvas (HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext + toDataURL) since jsdom lacks canvas. Alternatively, factor capture.ts so the testable native-mapping logic is a pure function (source->CameraSource, opts mapping) tested under node, and treat the fileToThumbnail web round-trip as manual/integration only.

Make platform detection injectable/dynamic in capture.ts (e.g. import { Capacitor } and call isNativePlatform() at runtime, or accept an override) so the native-path test can vi.mock('@capacitor/core'); do not rely on runtime.ts's cached const. Add the vi.mock pattern to the test plan explicitly.

Add gateFacePhoto handling to the spec: the native capture flow must ensure a face_processing consent record exists (call the consent endpoint / reuse the web consent recording path) before /api/generate-avatar, and the testPlan must list 'expect 403 if face_processing consent not recorded' as a known case.

Rewrite the ProfileEditDrawer edit to add an explicit native dataUrl branch: when capture returns a dataUrl, skip fileToThumbnail, set photoUrl to the (already-downscaled) dataUrl, decide uploadChildPhoto behavior, keep setAvatarMeta(undefined). State this is logic, not a signature widen.

Reconcile capture.ts return type with consumers: have AvatarCreator call capture and feed the dataUrl straight to setRefPhoto (bypassing onPickPhoto(file)), and have ProfileEditDrawer handle both File (web input) and dataUrl (native) paths explicitly.

Fix every line-number reference: AvatarCreator photo input is L190 (not 176), consent checkbox L182 / photo label L188 (not 168/176); /api/generate-avatar is L854 with validation at L870-884 (not 743/759-773); env CORS origins are in src/config/env.ts L95-96 (NOT src/server/env.ts); ProfileEditDrawer file input L162 / avatarMeta=undefined L46 are correct.
Update file_paths.xml step to verify-only: a cache-path entry already exists (<cache-path name="my_cache_images" path="."/>), so no creation is needed.

Specify exact Android manifest lines: <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.CAMERA"/>, <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.READ_MEDIA_IMAGES"/>, and <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" android:maxSdkVersion="32"/>.
Add the two Info.plist keys concretely (NSCameraUsageDescription, NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription) with the privacy-contract copy; current Info.plist has neither.

specs.title: Avatar hosting: enable Firebase Storage + migrate off inline data URLs

spec.title: Avatar hosting: enable Firebase Storage + migrate off inline data URLs
spec.goal: Stop persisting AI-generated child avatars (and uploaded photos) as multi-hundred-KB base64 data: URLs inside the Firestore ChildProfile document. Enable/confirm Firebase Storage on arborprd-westeu, route every generated avatar through the existing uploadChildPhoto() object-storage path (so photoUrl holds a short https Storage download URL), fix the CSP that will otherwise block those URLs, deploy the storage.rules (which the prod pipeline currently never ships), and one-time migrate existing inline-data-URL avatars to Storage objects. Net effect: smaller/cheaper Firestore docs, no doc-bloat or 1MB-limit risk, avatars that render cross-device (HeroAvatar/EmotionAvatar) instead of silently downgrading, and a defined cost/retention posture for the bucket.

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\scripts\migrate-avatars-to-storage.ts — one-time Node migration (firebase-admin): iterate users/*/children/* docs, detect photoUrl.startsWith('data:'), upload bytes to users/{uid}/children/{childId}/avatar/{ts}.<ext> via admin Storage bucket, rewrite photoUrl to the public/download URL, leave avatar metadata intact; dry-run flag + per-doc logging + idempotent (skip non-data: URLs)

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\profile\ProfileEditDrawer.tsx — in onCreated (L246-249) upload the generated dataUrl via uploadChildAvatar() before setPhotoUrl; on failure fall back to the inline dataUrl (graceful degradation, mirror onPickPhoto L42-44); keep avatarMeta set

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\profile\ProfileEditDrawer.tsx — HOT: cited by web-avatar + privacy lenses; onCreated/onPickPhoto are the contended lines, scope the edit to the upload call only

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\.github\workflows\arbor-deploy.yml — shared deploy pipeline; single-line --only change, coordinate so a concurrent mission doesn't also rewrite the deploy step
apiChanges: No new HTTP routes. /api/generate-avatar (api.ts L743) is unchanged — it still returns { dataUrl } and still never stores the reference photo. The data:->Storage conversion happens client-side in ProfileEditDrawer via the existing firebase/storage SDK (direct-to-bucket upload, not through the Express API).
CSP response-header change (server-emitted via helmet in createApp.ts): imgSrc and connectSrc gain firebasestorage hosts — affects all hosted (non-local) responses.
dataModel: No schema change to ChildProfile (types.ts L1-20). photoUrl semantics shift from 'usually a data: URL for generated avatars' to 'an https Firebase Storage download URL' — the field/type is already documented to allow both (L10-12). avatar metadata block (style/source/createdAt, L14-19) is unchanged.

Optional (recommend, low cost): add avatar.storagePath?: string to types.ts so old objects can be deleted on replace/child-delete and to make migration idempotent. If added, set it in ProfileEditDrawer.onCreated and the migration script.
dependencies: Firebase Storage default bucket must be ENABLED on project arborprd-westeu (region europe-west4 to match Firestore/Cloud Run). Bucket name should resolve to the VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET the client already builds with: <project>.firebasestorage.app (default in arbor-deploy.yml L100). Verify the live bucket id matches before migrating.
firebase-admin (dev/script dependency) for the migration script — confirm present or add to app/package.json.
tsx or ts-node to run the TS migration script.
Deploy credentials already in CI (WIF or GCP_SA_KEY) need Storage Admin / Firebase Admin to push storage rules and (for the migration) read/write the bucket + Firestore.

Bucket CORS config (gsutil/gcloud) allowing GET from https://arborprd-westeu.web.app, https://arborprd-westeu.firebaseapp.com, capacitor://localhost, https://localhost so native webviews can fetch avatar objects (mirrors env.ts corsOrigins L87-97).
steps: 1. Enable the Firebase Storage default bucket on arborprd-westeu in europe-west4; confirm the bucket id equals <project>.firebasestorage.app (the value the client build already injects). Document in docs/ops/storage-enablement.md.
2. Add uploadChildAvatar() to app/src/lib/storage.ts (avatar/ path, contentType from mime, same throw-on-unavailable contract as uploadChildPhoto).
3. Wire ProfileEditDrawer.onCreated to call uploadChildAvatar() and only fall back to the inline dataUrl on failure; (optional) record avatar.storagePath.
4. Extend cspDirectives() in createApp.ts: add https://firebasestorage.googleapis.com + https://*.firebasestorage.app to imgSrc and connectSrc.
5. Add onError->ArborMascot fallback in HeroAvatar.tsx for dead Storage URLs.
6. Confirm/tighten storage.rules (image content-type, 5MB cap, per-uid ownership) and change arbor-deploy.yml deploy to --only hosting,firestore,storage.
7. Configure bucket CORS for web + native origins.
8. Write scripts/migrate-avatars-to-storage.ts (firebase-admin, dry-run first, idempotent, logs counts).
9. Run lint + tests + build (tsc) locally; add storage.test.ts.
10. Deploy via the normal pipeline (API redeploy carries no env change for this mission; hosting+rules+storage rules ship together).
11. Run the migration in dry-run, review, then for-real against prod; spot-check that photoUrl on a few children is now an https URL and HeroAvatar/EmotionAvatar render.
12. Set a bucket lifecycle/retention policy (cost + COPPA-adjacent retention) and document it.
testPlan: Unit: storage.test.ts — uploadChildAvatar derives users/{uid}/children/{childId}/avatar/{ts}.<ext>, passes contentType, throws when storage is undefined (mock firebase/storage).
Unit/integration: migration script in dry-run against a seeded emulator/fixture — converts only data: URLs, leaves https URLs untouched, is idempotent on re-run.

Manual web (prod-like build, CSP ON): generate an avatar in ProfileEditDrawer -> network shows a Storage PUT, saved photoUrl is https://...firebasestorage..., no CSP violation in console; reload on a different browser/device shows the avatar (proves no longer device-local).
Manual: upload-a-photo path (onPickPhoto) still works and also yields a Storage URL.
Manual native (Capacitor iOS/Android once built): avatar image loads in the webview (validates bucket CORS for capacitor://localhost / https://localhost).
Negative: simulate Storage unavailable -> ProfileEditDrawer falls back to inline dataUrl without crashing; HeroAvatar onError falls back to Sprout for a bad URL.
Rules: firebase emulator rules test — a user cannot write under another uid's path; >5MB rejected; non-image content-type rejected (if enforced).
Regression: npm run lint && npm test && npm run build all green.
risks: storage.rules has never been deployed by the prod pipeline (deploy is --only hosting,firestore). If the bucket is enabled with default/locked rules, client uploads may currently fail; shipping the storage rules is a hard prerequisite, not optional.
Bucket id mismatch: if the actual enabled bucket is <project>.appspot.com but the client builds with <project>.firebasestorage.app (or vice versa), uploads/reads break. Verify before migrating.

CSP regression: forgetting the connectSrc entry (not just imgSrc) blocks the SDK's upload/download XHR. Both must be added. Local dev hides this (CSP off when arborEnv=local), so it only surfaces in prod.
Migration is mutating prod Firestore docs in place — must be idempotent, dry-run-first, and back up (export) the users collection before running. A bad rewrite could blank avatars.

Old inline data: URLs already in Firestore keep working in-app even before migration (Avatar/HeroAvatar render data: fine), so there is no user-visible breakage if migration is delayed — but doc bloat persists.

Cost: enabling Storage adds storage + egress + (negligible) op costs. Without a lifecycle policy, replaced avatars accumulate forever; the current uploadChildPhoto path is timestamped and never deletes old objects — add cleanup-on-replace or a TTL.

Privacy/retention (cross-cutting): photos/avatars now live as durable Storage objects with no deletion lifecycle; child-delete (ProfileEditDrawer.handleDelete) deletes Firestore data but NOT the Storage objects — a separate cleanup is needed to honor deletion (note for the COPPA/retention mission, do not silently leave orphaned faceprints).
generate-avatar route is still unmetered/cost-unbounded (separate mission) — this mission does not address image-gen spend, only storage of the result.
spec.deployNotes: Frontend + rules change, plus a one-time data migration; no Cloud Run env change is strictly required for M2 (CSP is emitted by the already-deployed Express app, but the cspDirectives edit ships in the API image, so the API DOES need a redeploy for the new CSP to take effect). Order: (1) enable bucket + set CORS + lifecycle; (2) merge code; (3) pipeline deploys API (new CSP), client, and rules — change arbor-deploy.yml to --only hosting,firestore,storage so storage.rules ships; (4) run the migration script with prod admin creds after rules are live. VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET is already wired in the build (arbor-deploy.yml L100) defaulting to <project>.firebasestorage.app — confirm it matches the enabled bucket. Verify post-deploy: generate an avatar, confirm photoUrl is an https firebasestorage URL with no CSP console errors, and that it renders on a second device.

missingPieces: Avatar.tsx (C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\ui\Avatar.tsx) is NOT in filesToEdit, but it is the PRIMARY child-photo renderer — it is the component shown in ProfileEditDrawer L149 and renders photoURL as a raw (L40-47) with NO onError fallback. Once avatars become https Storage URLs that can 404/expire, a dead URL here shows a broken image. The spec only hardens HeroAvatar.tsx. Avatar.tsx needs the same onError->initials fallback.

EmotionAvatar does not exist anywhere in the codebase (grep over all .tsx returns zero hits; it appears only in PRD/doc markdown). The spec's stated net effect ('avatars that render ... HeroAvatar/EmotionAvatar') and testPlan ('HeroAvatar/EmotionAvatar render') reference a non-existent component. Either it must be created in a sibling mission first or the reference is hand-waving.

storage.test.ts is listed but no vitest setup for mocking firebase/storage is described; the existing test runner is vitest (scripts/vitest.config.mjs) — fine, but the spec should state the mock approach concretely (vi.mock('firebase/storage')) since storage.ts imports ref/uploadString/getDownloadURL at module top.

The migration script path assumption is correct (users/{uid}/children/{childId}, confirmed in ProfileContext.tsx L68/L124) BUT firebase-admin's default bucket resolution must be set explicitly — admin.storage().bucket() defaults to <project>.appspot.com unless storageBucket is passed to initializeApp or GCLOUD_STORAGE_BUCKET is set. The spec does not call out that the script must pass the .firebasestorage.app bucket name explicitly, which is the exact bucket-id-mismatch risk it warns about elsewhere.

No mention of updating the .workspace/ mirror tree (a full duplicate of the app exists at C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\.workspace\PPPPtherapy-\app). If that is a live worktree, edits must land in the canonical tree only — worth a one-line note given buildLevel=worktree.
conflicts: createApp.ts cspDirectives() — HOT shared file. The spec itself flags this; M2 only appends to imgSrc/connectSrc arrays (L39, L42-50), which is append-only and merge-friendly, but any sibling mission that also rewrites cspDirectives() (e.g. a Stripe/billing CSP mission adding js.stripe.com) will textually collide. Coordinate as a single combined CSP edit.

ProfileEditDrawer.tsx — HOT, cited by web-avatar + privacy/COPPA lenses. M2 edits onCreated (L246-249); a COPPA/delete mission edits handleDelete (L87-101). Different functions, low collision risk, but both add imports at top (L12 region) — watch the import block.

arbor-deploy.yml L111 single-line --only change — any concurrent mission rewriting the deploy step (e.g. adding --only ...,functions) will conflict. Spec correctly flags this; must be the only mission touching L111 in a wave.
safetyGaps: COPPA/retention: child-delete (ProfileEditDrawer.handleDelete -> deleteChild) deletes Firestore data but NOT Storage objects. After M2, faces/avatars become DURABLE Storage objects that survive a deletion request — this is a regression in deletion completeness for child face data. The spec acknowledges this and defers it to 'the COPPA/retention mission', but shipping Storage persistence WITHOUT the deletion path creates orphaned child-face objects in the window between missions. At minimum M2 should add a best-effort deleteObject on child-delete and on avatar-replace, or the mission should be explicitly sequenced AFTER (not before) the deletion-cleanup work.

Cost: no lifecycle/TTL is enforced by code — it is a manual gcloud step in the runbook (step 12). uploadChildPhoto/uploadChildAvatar are timestamped and never delete the prior object, so every avatar regeneration accumulates objects forever. Without avatar.storagePath tracking (which the spec lists as merely 'optional'), cleanup-on-replace is impossible. The storagePath field should be REQUIRED, not optional, for this mission to have any deletion/cost story.

a11y: HeroAvatar onError fallback to ArborMascot is fine, but Avatar.tsx (the gap above) on error would show browser-default broken-image with the alt text — acceptable but the initials fallback is better; ensure the onError swaps to the initials span, not just hides.
Migration mutates prod Firestore in place — spec says back up/export first but does not make the export a hard gated step in the runbook ordering; should be step 0, blocking.
corrections: Add C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\ui\Avatar.tsx to filesToEdit: add onError on the (L40) to fall back to the initials span, mirroring the HeroAvatar fix. This is the primary renderer in the drawer and elsewhere.
Remove or correct every 'EmotionAvatar' reference (net-effect statement and testPlan) — the component does not exist. Replace with Avatar/HeroAvatar, the components that actually render photoUrl.

Fix the package.json dependency note: firebase-admin is ALREADY a regular dependency (app/package.json L36, ^13.10.0) — do NOT add it as a devDependency. Only add the 'migrate:avatars' tsx script. tsx is already a devDependency (L61).

Make avatar.storagePath REQUIRED (set in ProfileEditDrawer.onCreated, uploadChildAvatar return, and the migration) so replace/delete cleanup and idempotency are actually possible — otherwise the cost and COPPA-deletion stories are unachievable.

In migrate-avatars-to-storage.ts, explicitly pass the storageBucket (<project>.firebasestorage.app) to admin.initializeApp() — firebase-admin defaults to <project>.appspot.com, which is the exact mismatch the spec warns about and would silently write to the wrong bucket.

Sequence M2 AFTER (or bundle with) the Storage-deletion-on-child-delete work, or add a best-effort deleteObject to handleDelete and avatar-replace within M2 itself, so durable child-face objects are never orphaned by a deletion request.

State the storage.test.ts mock strategy concretely (vi.mock('firebase/storage')) and add a guard test that uploadChildAvatar throws 'storage-unavailable' when storage is undefined (the existing contract).
Make 'firestore export/backup of users collection' an explicit gated step 0 in storage-enablement.md before any in-place migration.

buildLevel 'worktree' is appropriate (multi-file frontend+backend+rules+CI+script with a prod data migration). Keep it. effort 'M' is slightly light given the migration + bucket enablement + CORS + lifecycle ops work; M/L boundary, acceptable.

specs.title: Apply earned cosmetic frame to <Avatar> globally

spec.title: Apply earned cosmetic frame to Avatar globally
spec.goal: Thread the child's earned cosmetics.activeFrame (practice/cosmetics.ts) into ui/Avatar.tsx as an optional, presentational frame badge, and apply it at the child-identity Avatar render sites for the ACTIVE child via a single context-sourced hook — with zero per-site practice-data plumbing. Parent/user avatars (Sidebar, CoachTab) are deliberately left unframed.
filesToCreate: C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\practice\useActiveChildFrame.ts
C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\practice\cosmeticStats.ts
C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\ui\Avatar.test.tsx
filesToEdit: C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\ui\Avatar.tsx
C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\profile\ProfileSwitcher.tsx
C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\profile\ProfileEditDrawer.tsx
C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\components\profile\RewardsCard.tsx
dataModel: No persisted schema change. New presentational type on Avatar: frame?: { emoji: string; label: string } | null (derived at runtime from cosmetics.Cosmetic, never stored). cosmetics.CosmeticState.activeFrame (Cosmetic|null) is the source of truth — already exists in practice/cosmetics.ts (L52-53,70-71).
dependencies: None. Pure frontend; reuses existing context/ProfileContext.tsx (useProfile -> activeChild), practice/usePracticeData.ts, practice/cosmetics.ts (evaluateCosmetics/activeFrame). No new npm deps, no env vars, no backend, no Cloud Run redeploy.
steps: 1. Extract stat mapping into a pure helper to avoid duplication: create practice/cosmeticStats.ts exporting `cosmeticStatsFromPracticeData(data: PracticeData): CosmeticStats` containing the exact logic currently inlined in RewardsCard.tsx L13-22 (totalSessions = speech+mimic+adventures+events items + completed missions; streakDays = data.streak; domainsTouched = data.week.domainsTouched.length). Import PracticeData type from ./usePracticeData and CosmeticStats from ./cosmetics.

2. Refactor RewardsCard.tsx to call the new helper (replace the inline useMemo body at L13-22 with `useMemo(() => cosmeticStatsFromPracticeData(data), [data])`) so behavior is byte-identical and there is a single source of truth. Keep evaluateCosmetics usage unchanged.

3. Create practice/useActiveChildFrame.ts: a hook with no args that reads `const { activeChild } = useProfile()`, calls `usePracticeData(activeChild.id)`, derives stats via cosmeticStatsFromPracticeData, runs evaluateCosmetics, and returns `state.activeFrame` mapped to `{ emoji, label } | null` (return null when activeFrame is null). This is THE no-plumbing seam: any child-identity site calls one hook, gets the frame from context.

4. Edit ui/Avatar.tsx: add optional prop `frame?: { emoji: string; label: string } | null` to the props type. When `frame` is truthy, wrap BOTH the and the initials branches in a relative-positioned container and overlay a corner badge: an absolutely-positioned (bottom-right, e.g. style bottom/right: -2, small rounded pill) rendering `frame.emoji` as aria-hidden plus an sr-only label `${name}'s ${frame.label} reward` (visually-hidden via existing tailwind sr-only). Size the badge proportionally to `size` (e.g. round(size*0.42)). Keep the no-frame path returning exactly today's markup (zero visual change) so untouched call sites are unaffected. Respect reduced motion: badge is static, no animation.

5. Wire ProfileSwitcher.tsx: import useActiveChildFrame; call it once at top of component; pass `frame={frame}` to the ACTIVE child trigger Avatar (L19). For the dropdown list (L57) only pass `frame` when `p.id === activeChild.id` (else undefined) — do NOT fetch per-child data for non-active rows.

6. Wire ProfileEditDrawer.tsx header Avatar (L149, size 56): pass the active child's frame. Reuse useActiveChildFrame (the drawer always edits the active child). Leave onPickPhoto/onCreated logic untouched.
7. Leave Sidebar.tsx (L71, parent/user) and CoachTab.tsx (L373, parent/user) UNFRAMED — they render the signed-in PARENT, not the child; no edit needed. Document this in the Avatar.tsx prop JSDoc.

8. Create Avatar.test.tsx: assert (a) no frame -> renders single img/span, no badge node; (b) frame given with photoURL -> img still renders AND badge emoji present + sr-only label text; (c) frame given with initials fallback (no photoURL) -> initials + badge; (d) sr-only label includes name and frame.label.
9. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and the vitest suite; confirm existing cosmetics.test.ts still green and new Avatar.test.tsx passes.
testPlan: Unit: new Avatar.test.tsx (4 cases above) under app/src/components/ui/.
Unit: existing app/src/practice/cosmetics.test.ts must remain green (activeFrame selection unchanged).
Unit (recommended): a test for cosmeticStatsFromPracticeData verifying totalSessions/streak/domainsTouched mapping matches the old inline RewardsCard math.
Regression: full `npx tsc --noEmit` (Avatar prop is optional so all ~7 existing call sites compile unchanged).

Manual web (npm run dev): a child with >=1 session shows a frame badge on ProfileSwitcher active avatar + ProfileEditDrawer header; a brand-new child (0 sessions, activeFrame null) shows NO badge; switching child updates the badge; parent avatar in Sidebar/Coach shows no badge.

risks: Per-child frames in the ProfileSwitcher dropdown are intentionally out of scope (would require N usePracticeData subscriptions = the exact plumbing the mission forbids). Only the active child is framed there. State this explicitly to the reviewer.

specs.title: Memory-portrait / hero-card export + Trusted Sharing activation

spec.title: Memory-portrait / hero-card export + Trusted Sharing activation
spec.goal: Let a parent compose a watermarked celebration "hero card" entirely client-side on a <canvas> — the child's existing stylized hero avatar (useHeroAvatar) + a chosen milestone/win + a reward badge — then download it and share it via the native share sheet / Web Share API, surfaced from the existing Trusted Sharing section. No AI image-generation, no new backend cost-bearing route, so none of the hot backend files (api.ts, createApp.ts, modelRouter.ts, usage.ts, env.ts) are touched.

steps: 1. Build lib/heroCard.ts: port the image-load + object-cover scaling math from lib/image.ts (L14-29) and the gradient-ring look from HeroAvatar (L52-53); draw portrait, child name, milestone/win line, reward badge chip, and a non-removable 'Made with Arbor' watermark; accept lang to right-align HE text and mirror layout. Return a PNG Blob.

2. Build lib/shareImage.ts: feature-detect navigator.canShare({files:[file]}); if supported call navigator.share, else fall back to the anchor-download pattern (HeroComicsTab L57-63). For native (isNativePlatform from lib/runtime), dynamic-import @capacitor/filesystem to write the blob to Directory.Cache and @capacitor/share to open the sheet — all wrapped in try/catch no-ops like initNativeShell (native.ts L13-37).

3. Build HeroCardComposer.tsx: if !hasHero (useHeroAvatar), show the same 'Create {name}'s hero' invite as HeroComicsTab L66-82 routing to the profile tab. Otherwise: milestone picker (checked milestones), badge picker (evaluateCosmetics), live canvas preview re-rendered on selection change, Save + Share buttons. All copy via t(). If modal: add role=dialog, focus trap, Escape-to-close, return-focus (fixes the AvatarCreator a11y gap, do not repeat it).
4. Wire into TrustedSharing.tsx: add a 'Celebration card' SectionCard that opens the composer; on success push to setAudit (L33).
5. Add herocard.* keys to i18n.ts EN + HE maps (parity with sec.sharing.* at L294/L783). Use {name} interpolation like sec.sharing.sub.
6. Add @capacitor/share + @capacitor/filesystem to package.json; run npm i && npx cap sync.
7. Edit Info.plist (NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription) and verify AndroidManifest FileProvider authority post-sync.
8. Add track('hero_card_created',{milestoneId,badge,platform}) and track('hero_card_shared',...) via lib/analytics (track signature analytics.ts L27) to make the sharing growth-loop measurable.
9. Write heroCard.test.ts; run tsc + vitest.
10. Manual web verify (download + share-sheet), then native build/verify per deployNotes.
testPlan: tsc --noEmit clean; full vitest suite green (repo runs 241 tests per memory).
heroCard.test.ts: composeHeroCard produces a non-empty PNG; HE lang right-aligns; watermark text always present; graceful path when hero image fails to load (draws placeholder, never throws).
shareImage.test.ts: with navigator.canShare mocked true -> navigator.share called; mocked absent -> anchor.click() download fallback taken.

Web manual: open Trusted Sharing -> Celebration card, pick a checked milestone + a reward, preview renders, Save downloads PNG, Share opens browser share sheet (Chrome/Edge desktop will fall back to download; mobile Safari/Chrome shows sheet).
a11y manual: keyboard-only open/close, focus trapped, Escape closes, focus returns; verify with no color-only state (label + icon).
Native (after cap sync + device/sim): card composes inside WebceView canvas (no camera/mic perms needed), Share sheet opens, 'Save to Photos' works on iOS (Info.plist string present) and Android.
Verify the canvas approach needs NO network (works offline) — distinguishes M4 from the AI image surfaces that break on native CDN/CORS.
risks: A Google/uploaded remote photoUrl (not a data: URL) may taint the canvas (CORS) and break toBlob/toDataURL. Mitigation: set img.crossOrigin='anonymous' + referrerPolicy='no-referrer'; if the draw taints, fall back to compositing over the seeded ArborMascot/illustration instead of the raw photo (also dodges the privacy concern of putting a real face on a shareable card). Prefer the stylized hero; never the raw face photo.
iOS cannot be built/archived without a Mac (per platformGaps) — native share path ships unverified on iOS until a Mac build; gate the native code behind isNativePlatform so web is unaffected.
Stale committed dist/ under ios/App/App/public can ship an old frontend if npx cap sync is skipped — must run cap sync after adding plugins.
navigator.share file support is uneven on desktop browsers; the anchor-download fallback must always be present so 'Share' never dead-ends.
i18n: in-game/card body strings are otherwise hardcoded EN across the app — adding HE keys here is required, not optional, for the Israel-first GTM; missing HE parity would regress the RTL surface.
Scope creep: do NOT add a generated/AI image, server persistence of bytes, or a new /api route — that would pull in the hot backend files M4 is designed to avoid.
spec.deployNotes: "Two-part deploy. (1) Frontend-only for web: build the Vite app and deploy to Firebase Hosting (arborprd-westeu.web.app) — no Cloud Run redeploy, no backend, no new env, because there is no API change. (2) Native: npm i the two Capacitor plugins, npx cap sync ios android, edit Info.plist (NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription), then build Android (APK/AAB) and iOS archive (iOS requires a Mac). Verify offline behavior (canvas works with no network) and the share sheet on each platform. No CORS/env.ts change needed since no native AI call is added by this mission."

risks: api.ts and createApp.ts are THE hot files (every AI route + every guard list) — concurrent missions WILL conflict here; isolate edits to clearly-scoped blocks and rebase carefully.
base64 data URLs in Firestore docs risk the 1MB doc-size limit for large panels — may force Cloud Storage offload (flagged, out of scope) ; mitigate by storing downscaled art or a storageRef.

Quota too aggressive could block legitimate replay; cache-hit path must NOT count against the image quota (only true generations decrement).
entitlements default to plus when ENFORCE_ENTITLEMENTS=false (beta) so 402 won't fire in beta — verify behavior under both flags.

spec.deployNotes: backend+frontend. Deploy backend FIRST (Cloud Run via cloudbuild.prod.yaml, project arborprd-westeu / europe-west4) so the server-side cache + image quota + new /generate-scene contract exist before the web/native clients call them. Set Cloud Run env: IMAGE_USER_DAILY_LIMIT, FREE_IMAGES_PER_DAY (and optional DAILY_IMAGE_SPEND_CEILING). Configure Firestore TTL policies on sceneArt.expireAt and aiQuota.expireAt. Then deploy web (firebase hosting → https://arborprd-westeu.web.app). For native, run npx cap sync (ios+android) to ship the rebuilt bundle; no new native permissions required for this mission (art reuses the existing profile avatar). Verify: cache hit returns {cached:true} with no model call; Free user hits 402 over allotment; image-unit usage shows in the rollup.

missingPieces: createImageQuota signature is underspecified/wrong: to emit 402 {upgrade:{feature:'imageGenerate',plan:'plus'}} for Free vs 429 for Plus it MUST resolve entitlements. aiQuota.ts (the cited template) takes only (counters) and has no plan awareness — it can only ever 429. The middleware must take (entitlementStore, counters) and call resolveEntitlement like createCoachMeter (entitlements.ts:237-259). The filesToCreate description ('createImageQuota(counters)') and createApp step 5 ('createImageQuota(counters)') are both wrong.

Missing edit to the ApiDeps type in api.ts (lines 31-42). The spec passes sceneArtStore into createApiRouter deps but never lists adding `sceneArtStore: SceneArtStore` to the ApiDeps type and the destructure at api.ts:75 — without it this is a build-blocking TS error.

Body-size limit not addressed: /generate-scene and /generate-comic validate avatar dataUrls up to 6MB (api.ts:943, 993) but the global parser is express.json({limit:'250kb'}) (createApp.ts:115); only /api/vision gets 12mb (L114). A real avatar dataUrl is rejected with 413 before reaching the handler. The mission needs a raised JSON limit for the image routes (e.g. app.use(['/api/generate-scene','/api/generate-comic','/api/generate-avatar'], express.json({limit:'8mb'})) mounted before the 250kb default), or the cache cannot be exercised with a real avatar.

FREE allotment plumbing: the spec adds freeImagesPerDay to env.ts/PLAN-style config but never specifies where the Free vs abuse-ceiling thresholds live relative to PLAN_LIMITS (entitlements.ts:41-63). PLAN_LIMITS has no image field today; either add imagesPerDay to PlanLimits or read freeImagesPerDay/imageUserDailyLimit straight from env — the spec leaves this ambiguous.

i18n: spec references both 'src/context/LanguageContext usage' and 'src/lib/i18n.ts'. The real strings live in src/lib/i18n.ts (confirmed: gen.adventure.fail, prac.adventures.title). The LanguageContext path is noise; the edit target is lib/i18n.ts only (HE+EN keys).

recordImageUsage must extend the TokenUsage/recordUsage seam without breaking normalizeUsage: usage.ts:43-49 early-returns when normalizeUsage is null, so image-unit logging needs its own code path (not a fake token shape) plus a matching rollup metric in usageRollup.ts (recordUsageRollup) — usageRollup.ts is NOT in filesToEdit but recordImageUsage will need it to surface in the rollup.
conflicts: api.ts is THE hot file: /generate-scene/comic/avatar handlers (L854-1067), generate-adventure (L1069), gateFacePhoto (L83) and the ApiDeps type (L31) all live here. Any sibling avatar/consent/adventure mission editing the same handlers will collide. Isolate the cache-key + cache read/write to the /generate-scene block (L923-967) and the ApiDeps type edit.

createApp.ts middleware chain (L99-129) is shared with every quota/entitlement/gate mission. This mission inserts a new createImageQuota app.use AND a raised express.json body limit AND a sceneArtStore construction + dep injection at L129 — three edits in the same 30-line block that any sibling touching the guard list will clobber.

HeroScenePlayer.tsx uses api.generateComic with cache key `comic|${seed}|${shortHash(avatar)}` (L71-92), NOT generateScene. The spec says 'route its art generation through api.generateScene' — that is factually wrong for this component and would change the rendered art (comic panel vs scene). The shared persisted cache must use a disjoint key scope so HeroJourney(comic) and Adventures(scene) do not collide — the spec flags this risk but the filesToEdit description mis-describes the call.
env.ts (L82-135 config object + ArborConfig type L5-51) is touched by every config-adding mission; the 3 new fields must be added in a clearly-scoped block to avoid rebase clobber.
modelRouter.ts generateImage edits at L247 (GeminiDev) and L333 (VertexGemini) — both already call recordUsage; any mission touching the image model wiring will conflict here.
safetyGaps: Generated images bypass the output safety screen (only the text imagePrompt is screened via screenForImmediateEscalation at api.ts:929). The spec acknowledges this as a known cross-cutting gap and says 'do not regress' — acceptable, but persisting unscreened generated art into a per-child Firestore cache (sceneArt/{childUid}) makes a bad-tone image durable and re-served, which is a slight regression vs the current ephemeral per-tab map. Note this explicitly.

Beta entitlement reality: resolveEntitlement returns plan:'plus' for everyone when ENFORCE_ENTITLEMENTS=false (entitlements.ts:200-201), which is the current beta default (prod-only enforcement at L102). So the Free 402 path will NOT fire in beta — the cost guard's Free allotment is effectively a no-op until billing/enforcement is on. The 429 abuse ceiling DOES still apply (counter-only). Spec lists this in risks but should treat 'verify under both flags' as a required test, not a nice-to-have.

base64-in-Firestore exceeds the 1MB document limit for typical generated PNG scene art (these are routinely >1MB). Firestore .set() will THROW on oversize docs. quotaStore's FirestoreCounterStore fails OPEN (try/catch, L82-85); the spec does NOT require sceneCache to mirror that fail-open behavior, so an oversize-doc write could 500 the /generate-scene request after a successful (paid) model call. Either require fail-open on cache put, or make storageRef (Cloud Storage) the default rather than 'flagged out of scope'.

Cache-key privacy/COPPA: cache docs keyed by avatarHash + scenarioId are fine, but expireAt TTL must actually be configured in GCP console for project arborprd-westeu or art accumulates indefinitely per child — this is a manual ops step the spec lists but is easy to miss and is a data-minimization (COPPA) obligation, not optional.
corrections: Change createImageQuota to take (entitlementStore: EntitlementStore, counters: UsageCounterStore) and resolve the actor's plan via resolveEntitlement (mirror createCoachMeter, entitlements.ts:237-259): Free over freeImagesPerDay → 402 with {error,details,upgrade:{feature:'imageGenerate',plan:'plus'}}; any plan over imageUserDailyLimit (abuse ceiling) → 429 with Retry-After. Set X-Image-Quota-Limit/Remaining headers. Update createApp step 5 and the filesToCreate description accordingly.

Add `sceneArtStore: SceneArtStore` to the ApiDeps type (api.ts:31-42) and to the destructure at api.ts:75, and import the type. Without this the createApiRouter call in createApp.ts (L129) won't typecheck.

Add a raised JSON body limit for the image routes in createApp.ts BEFORE the 250kb default (L115): app.use(['/api/generate-scene','/api/generate-comic','/api/generate-avatar'], express.json({limit:'8mb'})). Otherwise 6MB avatar references 413 before the handler and the whole hero-art path is dead. Add this to filesToEdit/steps.

Fix the HeroScenePlayer description: it calls api.generateComic with key `comic|${seed}|${shortHash(avatar)}`. Make the server cache key scope-prefixed and disjoint (e.g. `comic|...` vs `adv|...`) and keep the generateComic call — do not 'switch to generateScene'. Demote the module Map to L1 and add a read-through to the server cache on the comic path.

Make FirestoreSceneArtStore.put fail-open (try/catch + log, mirror quotaStore.ts:82-85) so an oversize/failed cache write never breaks a successful generation, AND default to a Cloud Storage storageRef (not inline base64) for scene PNGs >~900KB, since inline base64 will hit the 1MB Firestore doc limit. If staying base64-only for v1, downscale art server-side and document the size ceiling.

Add usageRollup.ts to filesToEdit: recordImageUsage needs a rollup metric path (image-units) alongside recordUsageRollup so image spend actually surfaces in the founder dashboard; logging an ai.usage line alone won't increment the rollup. Implement recordImageUsage as a distinct image-unit code path in usage.ts (do not fabricate token counts that normalizeUsage would mis-handle).

Add an integration test asserting the cache-HIT path does NOT call createImageQuota's increment / does NOT decrement the image quota (only true model generations count) — this is in risks but must be a hard test, and requires mounting order such that a cache hit short-circuits inside the handler AFTER the quota middleware; either move the cache check before the quota increment or have the handler refund/not-count on hit. Clarify ordering: as written (quota middleware mounted on the route, cache check inside the handler) every cache hit STILL increments the quota counter — that contradicts the stated requirement and must be fixed.
Correct i18n target to src/lib/i18n.ts only (drop the LanguageContext path); add HE+EN keys for the loading caption and fallback.

Clarify env wiring: define whether freeImagesPerDay lives on PLAN_LIMITS (entitlements.ts) or is read directly from env in imageQuota; PLAN_LIMITS currently has no image field, so either extend PlanLimits or keep it env-only and document that Family/Plus share the same abuse ceiling.

specs.title: Emotion-state avatar deepening (FeelingsLab + Today/Overview mood mirror)

spec.title: Emotion-state avatar deepening (FeelingsLab + Today/Overview mood mirror)
spec.goal: Let the child's avatar visibly mirror the mood the family has EXPLICITLY logged today, beyond FeelingsLab — surfaced on the Today/Overview status band and check-in card. Strictly depiction-only: the avatar reflects a parent/child-entered mood, never an inferred emotional state. No AI image generation, no classifier, no new cost. Also unify the emotion-avatar on the stylized hero identity (not the raw photo) so the child's face is consistent across surfaces.

risks: Two competing 'mood/emotion' signals (numeric wellness mood vs FeelingsLab string emotion ids). Spec deliberately mirrors the EXPLICIT wellness log only to honor 'depicted, not inferred'; do NOT wire FeelingsLab practice-event sentiment into the Today mirror or it becomes inference. Document this in mood.ts.

EmotionAvatar.tsx is also consumed by FeelingsLabTab (and indirectly referenced in Shell.tsx/PracticeHubTab.tsx per grep) — adding props must be backward-compatible (all new props optional) to avoid breaking those call sites.

Identity desync (memory: photoUrl overloaded by upload vs generated avatar; useHeroAvatar branches on photoUrl/avatar). Routing EmotionAvatar through useHeroAvatar inherits that desync; mitigated, not fixed here (full fix is the types.ts single-source work, out of scope).

Reduced-motion: app's global CSS guard does NOT cover motion/react JS transitions; EmotionAvatar's CSS transition-all is covered by CSS guard but verify the aura doesn't animate via JS. Keep the mirror CSS-only to stay within the existing guard.
i18n.ts is a hot shared file — append keys at the end of the EN and HE blocks; avoid reordering to prevent merge conflicts with parallel string-extraction missions.
OverviewTab.tsx is hot/large; confine the edit to the status-band avatar block (L179-186) to minimize conflict surface with other Today missions.
No cost/quota impact (no model calls) — explicitly do NOT add any generate-* call to keep this off the unmetered-image-route risk surface.
spec.deployNotes: Frontend-only. No backend, no API, no env, no Firestore rules change (wellness collection already read/written under the existing users/{uid}/children/{childId}/** path). No Cloud Run redeploy needed. Ship as a normal web build to arborprd-westeu (firebase deploy / existing cloudbuild.prod.yaml web target). For native, run `npx cap sync` and rebuild the Capacitor shells so the new bundle ships (no manifest/Info.plist edits required since this is render-only). Verify post-deploy: log a mood in Today's check-in and confirm the Overview avatar badge updates; confirm no badge appears for a child with no mood logged today.

missingPieces: useHeroAvatar IMPORT SOURCE is undocumented. The hook is real but it is NOT in src/hooks/ — it is exported from src/components/ui/HeroAvatar.tsx (lines 15-21). The spec's dependencies list calls it a 'hook' alongside useChildCollection/useArbor implying src/hooks; a builder will waste time or create a wrong import. Steps 4/5 must specify: import { useHeroAvatar } from '../ui/HeroAvatar' (or '../../components/ui/HeroAvatar' from a hook file).

useHeroAvatar takes NO arguments and reads childProfile from context internally (HeroAvatar.tsx:16). It returns { url, isGenerated, hasHero, name } — there is no per-id variant. MoodMirrorAvatar/EmotionAvatar cannot pass a child to it; the spec's phrasing 'useHeroAvatar url' is loose but workable. The contract should be stated so the builder does not try useHeroAvatar(childId).

MOOD_SCALE tone tokens are unspecified. The spec says reuse 'the EMOTION_TONE palette pattern from FeelingsLabTab', but EMOTION_TONE is keyed by emotion-id (happy/sad/angry...), NOT by numeric 1-5. A new explicit 1->5 tone map must be authored (e.g. 1=sky-ink/sad ... 5=yellow-ink/happy). The exact CSS var per mood level is missing — builder must invent it, which risks drift from the spec's intent.

i18n key INVENTORY is incomplete/handwavy. Step 8 says 'mood.* (... scale labels happy..sad x5), checkin.* (title/mood/sleep/appetite/saved/tapToLog), feelings emotionAvatar aria label' but does not enumerate the exact key names the components will call (e.g. mood.scale.1..5 vs mood.happy/mood.sad). Without an exact key list, the t() calls in 4 edited files and the HE additions cannot be verified to match. Provide the literal key strings + EN + HE values.

EmotionAvatar role='img' a11y change is under-specified: the spec says wrap in role='img' with aria-label from 'emotionLabel + name i18n string' but the current Avatar fallback span is already aria-hidden='true' (Avatar.tsx:51) and the photo already has alt. Adding an outer role='img' will create a nested/duplicate image semantics conflict (img-in-img) that screen readers mis-announce. Spec must say to set the inner Avatar img alt='' / keep aria-hidden and put the single accessible name on the wrapper only.

spec.title: SpeechCoach real phoneme ASR: SoapBox primary + Whisper fallback + COPPA voice consent + prod endpoint
spec.goal: Turn the scaffolded childAsr seam into a real, production-wired child-articulation scorer: map SoapBox Labs' actual phoneme response (primary), keep hosted Whisper as cloud fallback and on-device Web Speech as the floor, gate every cloud scoring call behind a server-enforced COPPA voice_processing consent grant (the M9 consent ledger already exists but is NOT applied to /score-utterance), add retry + usage telemetry around the vendor calls, set the prod env to actually enable a provider, and unblock the mic on native (Info.plist / AndroidManifest).
