apiChanges: POST /api/score-utterance now requires an active voice_processing consent grant for the child; on a miss it returns 451 { error, purpose:'voice_processing', childId, consentRequired:true } (via requireConsent middleware) BEFORE invoking the vendor — was previously unguarded
POST /api/score-utterance request body gains childId (used for consent resolution + usage telemetry); existing { target, sound, level, audio } unchanged
POST /api/score-utterance is added to the per-user hourly aiQuota allow-list in createApp.ts (was unmetered)
GET /api/score-utterance status response is unchanged ({ configured, provider }) but client should still treat configured:false as on-device-only

No new public route is strictly required — /api/consent POST/GET/DELETE already exist (api.ts L222-272); client just needs to call them. Optionally add a thin api.setConsent/listConsent wrapper in lib/api.ts
dataModel: No new server data type: the ConsentGrant model (sharing/consent.ts) with purpose 'voice_processing' already exists, is time-boxed (365-day default) and server-enforced via isConsentActive
childAsr.ts ChildScoreInput gains an optional childId field for telemetry; ChildScoreResult is unchanged (result/heard/confidence/provider)

Optional ArborConfig additions in env.ts: soapbox score thresholds (got>=0.8, almost>=0.5) made configurable via SOAPBOX_GOT_THRESHOLD / SOAPBOX_ALMOST_THRESHOLD; testConfig.ts must mirror any added field
ai.usage telemetry gains an ASR line: recordUsage({ route:'analysis_structured' or new 'asr_child', provider:'soapbox'|'whisper', model }, {...}) — currently ASR records nothing
dependencies: No new npm packages required for backend: uses global fetch/FormData/Blob (Node 18+) already used in childAsr.ts, plus existing withModelRetry (src/ai/modelRetry.ts) and recordUsage (src/ai/usage.ts)
External: a licensed SoapBox Labs account (SOAPBOX_API_URL + SOAPBOX_API_KEY) OR a hosted OpenAI-compatible Whisper endpoint (WHISPER_API_URL + WHISPER_API_KEY) — BLOCKED ON GUY to procure/provide
Confirm SoapBox's real REST contract (request shape + per-phoneme response schema) — the current scoreSoapbox body/response is a placeholder guess and MUST be validated against vendor docs
Secret Manager entries for SOAPBOX_API_KEY/WHISPER_API_KEY in project arborprd-westeu; grant the Cloud Run service account secretAccessor
iOS native build still needs a Mac (Capacitor cap sync + Xcode) — pre-existing blocker, only affects shipping the iOS plist change to a device
Depends on M9 consent stack already merged (sharing/consent.ts, server/requireConsent.ts, /api/consent routes) — confirmed present in repo
steps: 1. Confirm SoapBox vendor contract: get the real REST endpoint, auth header, request body, and phoneme/word response schema from SoapBox docs (or the licensed account). This drives the mapping in step 3.

2. childAsr.ts: extend ChildScoreInput with optional childId; wrap both scoreWhisper and scoreSoapbox fetch calls in withModelRetry(() => ...) for transient (429/503/network) resilience, mirroring claudeVertexProvider's status-on-error pattern.

3. childAsr.ts: replace the ADAPT-TO-VENDOR block in scoreSoapbox with the real response mapping — parse SoapBox's per-phoneme correctness/overall score into the 3-level scale (got/almost/missed) using configurable thresholds; populate heard/confidence; keep provider:'soapbox'.
4. childAsr.ts: after each successful score, emit recordUsage so ASR spend becomes visible in the daily rollup (today it records nothing); include provider + a stable route label.

5. api.ts: import is already present (requireConsent, consentStore). Apply requireConsent(consentStore, 'voice_processing') as middleware on POST /score-utterance (router.post('/score-utterance', requireConsent(consentStore,'voice_processing'), async (req,res)=>{...})). resolveChildId already reads body.childId/childProfile.id.

6. api.ts: thread childId from req.body into scoreChildUtterance input for telemetry. Keep the existing NotConfiguredError -> { configured:false } behavior so on-device fallback still works when no provider is set.
7. createApp.ts: add '/api/score-utterance' to the createAiQuota allow-list array (L116-119) so cloud ASR is rate/cost-metered per user like chat/vision.
8. env.ts: keep existing CHILD_ASR_PROVIDER/SOAPBOX_*/WHISPER_* fields; optionally add SOAPBOX_GOT_THRESHOLD/SOAPBOX_ALMOST_THRESHOLD with sane defaults; mirror in testConfig.ts.

9. lib/api.ts: add setConsent({childId,purpose,granted,retention}) and listConsent(childId) wrappers over /api/consent; extend scoreUtterance to pass childId and to detect 451/consentRequired responses.

10. lib/speechScorer.ts: accept childId; on a 451 consentRequired response, return a distinct signal (e.g. { needsConsent:true }) rather than swallowing in catch; otherwise unchanged fallback to on-device transcript.

11. VoiceConsentGate.tsx: build a small component/hook that lazily checks listConsent for an active voice_processing grant, and if absent renders a parent-facing consent card explaining a recording of the child's voice is sent to a kid-tuned scorer (no persistence), with grant/decline that calls setConsent.

12. SpeechCoachTab.tsx: before the first cloud-scored recording (level!=='story' path in rec.onstop / startRecording), ensure consent; pass childProfile.id into scoreUtterance; if scoreUtterance signals needsConsent, open VoiceConsentGate and keep the on-device + parent-scoring floor working meanwhile.

13. Native: add NSMicrophoneUsageDescription (+ NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription) to Info.plist and RECORD_AUDIO to AndroidManifest.xml so getUserMedia + Web Speech can prompt/work in the webviews. Run npx cap sync.

14. Tests: add childAsr.realmap.test.ts for the SoapBox/Whisper mapping + thresholds; add an api integration test that POST /score-utterance returns 451 without consent and scores with an active grant (reuse LocalConsentStore + buildConsentGrant pattern from requireConsent.test.ts).

15. Deploy backend via cloudbuild.prod.yaml with CHILD_ASR_PROVIDER + vendor secrets set (Secret Manager); redeploy is required for native CORS + the new env to take effect. Then build/deploy web (Firebase Hosting) and cap sync native shells.
testPlan: Unit: childAsr.realmap.test.ts — SoapBox high score maps to 'got', mid to 'almost', low to 'missed'; Whisper transcript flows through matchResult; confidence/heard populated; thresholds configurable.
Unit: withModelRetry wraps a 503-then-200 vendor response and succeeds; a persistent 4xx (bad request) does NOT retry.

Integration (supertest, createTestConfig + childAsrProvider override): POST /api/score-utterance with no consent -> 451 { consentRequired:true, purpose:'voice_processing' }; after buildConsentGrant voice_processing for the child -> 200 with a score; provider:'none' -> { configured:false } and no vendor call.
Integration: /api/score-utterance now counts against aiQuota (exceeding the hourly cap returns the quota error).
Telemetry: a successful cloud score emits exactly one ai.usage line with provider soapbox|whisper (assert via logger spy).
Client: speechScorer returns {source:'cloud'} when configured+consented; returns needsConsent on 451; falls back to on-device transcript on network error; parent scoring still saves regardless.
Manual web: SpeechCoach record -> first time prompts voice consent -> grant -> 'Arbor heard' shows cloud result; decline -> on-device/parent path still works.

Manual native (Android emulator at minimum): RECORD_AUDIO prompt appears; getUserMedia succeeds; note Web Speech SpeechRecognition may still be unsupported in WKWebView so cloud ASR (audio upload) is the native primary path — verify the audio upload path works while on-device transcript is absent.
tsc --noEmit + full vitest suite green (241+ existing tests must still pass).
risks: SoapBox real response schema is unknown until licensed — the mapping in step 3 is a best guess against placeholder shape; ship behind CHILD_ASR_PROVIDER so Whisper (validated) is the safe default if SoapBox isn't ready.

COPPA: cloud ASR uploads a child voiceprint (biometric PII) to a third party. The consent gate MUST be enforced server-side (it is, via requireConsent) — do NOT rely on the client check alone. Also confirm vendor DPA/no-train/region (EU) terms; this is policy, not just code.

api.ts and createApp.ts are the hottest shared files (touched by nearly every other mission) — build in a worktree/branch and rebase carefully; the edits here are additive (one middleware arg + one allow-list entry) to minimize conflict surface.
Adding /score-utterance to aiQuota could throttle a legitimate high-volume practice session (ASHA dosage ~50 reps); verify the 80/hr request cap is comfortable or scope a higher ASR-specific cap.

Native: even with RECORD_AUDIO/NSMicrophone, Web Speech SpeechRecognition is unreliable in WKWebView/Android WebView — the on-device transcript fallback may be absent on native, making cloud ASR effectively required there; ensure graceful behavior when neither cloud (no consent) nor on-device is available (parent scoring floor).

Env/secret misconfig: setting CHILD_ASR_PROVIDER=soapbox without valid SOAPBOX_API_URL/KEY makes childAsrConfigured() false -> silently degrades to on-device; add a boot-time log so this is observable.
Bundle id is still placeholder app.arbor.family and iOS has never been archived — the plist change can't be device-verified without a Mac; Android emulator covers the mic path in the meantime.
spec.deployNotes: backend+frontend+native. Order: (1) deploy backend (Cloud Run via cloudbuild.prod.yaml) with CHILD_ASR_PROVIDER and vendor creds set — use Secret Manager for SOAPBOX_API_KEY/WHISPER_API_KEY, not literals in the YAML; a redeploy is also what activates the consent gate on /score-utterance and the native CORS origins. (2) Build + deploy web to Firebase Hosting (project arborprd-westeu, https://arborprd-westeu.web.app) with the SpeechCoach + consent wiring. (3) npx cap sync, then rebuild Android (works) and iOS (needs a Mac) so the mic permission strings ship. Verification: GET /api/score-utterance returns {configured:true, provider:'soapbox'|'whisper'}; POST without consent returns 451; with an active voice_processing grant returns a score; check ai.usage rollup shows ASR lines; confirm aiQuota counts the route. Keep CHILD_ASR_PROVIDER=none as the rollback (degrades cleanly to on-device).

missingPieces: src/ai/modelRouter.ts is NOT in filesToEdit, but the spec's telemetry plan requires it: recordUsage takes provider:ProviderId ('gemini_dev'|'vertex_gemini'|'vertex_claude') and route:ModelRoute (closed union). 'asr_child' and 'soapbox'/'whisper' do not exist in those unions, so recordUsage({route:'asr_child',provider:'soapbox'}) will not typecheck (modelRouter.ts:91, usage.ts:43-46).

recordUsage cannot emit ASR usage as designed: normalizeUsage (usage.ts:21-40) only recognizes token-count shapes (promptTokenCount / input_tokens); a SoapBox/Whisper response carries no tokens, so normalizeUsage returns null and recordUsage returns early emitting NOTHING. The testPlan assertion 'emits exactly one ai.usage line with provider soapbox|whisper' would FAIL. A dedicated ASR telemetry log line (new logger.info call) is needed, not a recordUsage() call.

createApp.ts express.json body-limit is missed entirely. createApp.ts:114-115 mounts a 12mb parser ONLY for /api/vision; everything else (incl. /api/score-utterance) gets express.json({limit:'250kb'}). The route claims to accept 8MB audio (api.ts:825) but the body parser rejects anything >250kb FIRST — the cloud audio path is effectively dead today. The mission must add app.use('/api/score-utterance', express.json({limit:'12mb'})) BEFORE the 250kb default; this file edit/line is absent from the spec.

cloudbuild.prod.yaml Secret Manager mechanism is wrong/missing: the deploy step has NO --set-secrets flag. Cloud Run secret refs CANNOT live inside --set-env-vars (line 49 uses the packed ^@^ delimiter string). Putting SOAPBOX_API_KEY as a 'Secret Manager ref' in --set-env-vars is invalid gcloud; a separate '--set-secrets SOAPBOX_API_KEY=soapbox-key:latest' arg must be added to the args array.

VoiceConsentGate.tsx references api.setConsent, but lib/api.ts exposes grantConsent (api.ts:204), not setConsent. Either the client method must be renamed/added or the component will not compile. The spec inconsistently calls it both setConsent and grantConsent.

No env.ts wiring for SOAPBOX_GOT_THRESHOLD/SOAPBOX_ALMOST_THRESHOLD is specified beyond 'optional'; if added, ArborConfig (env.ts:5-51), loadConfig (env.ts:82-135) AND testConfig.ts (which currently omits soapboxApiUrl/Key/thresholds entirely — only sets childAsrProvider:'none' and whisperModel) must all be updated or tsc breaks. testConfig is listed but the exact required fields are not.
conflicts: api.ts is the hottest shared file in the codebase (chat, council, voice, vision, avatar, scene, comic, adventure, consent, shares, score-utterance all live here, 1765 lines). Any sibling mission editing api.ts will collide. M7's actual api.ts change is now near-zero (the gate already exists at L813) so conflict surface is small IF M7 doesn't rewrite the route — but the spec thinks it's adding the gate, which would clobber the existing gateVoice wiring.

createApp.ts shared edits (aiQuota allow-list L120-123 AND the new json-limit middleware) overlap with any mission touching middleware order. The json-limit add MUST be sequenced before the 250kb express.json (L115) — order-sensitive against siblings inserting body parsers.

env.ts + testConfig.ts + ArborConfig type are touched by many missions (M9 consent, billing, entitlements). Adding ASR threshold fields risks merge collisions in the ArborConfig type literal and the createTestConfig object.

modelRouter.ts (ProviderId/ModelRoute unions) — if M7 must extend these for ASR telemetry, that is a high-traffic type file shared with every AI route; extending the unions could ripple into exhaustive switches in modelRouter and usage.
safetyGaps: COPPA enforcement claim is misleading: the spec frames the consent gate as a NEW server-side protection, but it already exists (gateVoice at api.ts:86-88, L813). However the gate is scoped appliesWhen body.audio is present — fine — BUT the GET /score-utterance status route and the on-device path leak no audio, so that's OK. The genuine gap: there is no server-side proof the vendor (SoapBox/Whisper) is EU-resident / no-train / under DPA. Sending a child voiceprint (biometric PII) to a third party with only CHILD_ASR_PROVIDER=none as the guard is a policy gap, not just code — must confirm DPA + region before any provider is enabled in prod.

Client never surfaces consentRequired today: lib/api.ts request() (L70-84) turns a 451 into a generic Error, and speechScorer.ts catch{} (L70-72) silently swallows it and falls through to on-device. So a child whose parent has NOT consented currently gets silent on-device scoring with no consent prompt ever shown — the consent UX is absent end-to-end. This is the real defect M7 should fix; the spec correctly wants VoiceConsentGate but mis-describes the starting state.

Audio size/cost: route caps audio at 8MB (api.ts:825) but the 250kb json parser blocks it first (see missingPieces). Once the parser is widened to 12mb, a 12mb base64 audio upload per utterance against an ASHA ~50-rep session is a real egress/cost vector; the aiQuota 30/min IP cap (createApp.ts:104-112) + hourly aiQuota may be too coarse — needs an ASR-specific size/rate cap.
No a11y note for the consent card: VoiceConsentGate is a parent-facing legal prompt; spec gives no a11y/role/focus-trap guidance for a modal that blocks a child-facing flow.
corrections: FIX THE FALSE PREMISE: /score-utterance is ALREADY consent-gated (api.ts:813 gateVoice = requireConsent(consentStore,'voice_processing',{appliesWhen:req=>!!req.body?.audio}), defined L86-88). Do NOT re-add the middleware (that would double-gate or clobber). Re-scope the mission to: (a) make the gate's 451 reach the client, (b) build the consent UX. The body already carries childId via resolveChildId (childProfile.id), so 'thread childId' is largely done.

Surface 451: in lib/api.ts request() (L70-84), add a branch for res.status===451 that throws a typed ConsentRequiredError (mirroring PaywallError at L52-62) carrying {purpose, childId}. Then in speechScorer.ts (L57-73) catch that specific error and return a needsConsent signal instead of the blanket catch{} that currently swallows it.

Replace the recordUsage telemetry plan: do NOT call recordUsage for ASR (it no-ops on token-less responses and won't typecheck with provider:'soapbox'). Either (a) add a dedicated logger.info('asr.usage',{provider,route,childId,ms}) line in childAsr.ts, or (b) extend ProviderId/ModelRoute in modelRouter.ts AND add modelRouter.ts to filesToEdit AND give normalizeUsage an ASR-shaped branch. Update the testPlan assertion accordingly.

Add the body-parser fix to createApp.ts: app.use('/api/score-utterance', express.json({limit:'12mb'})) placed BEFORE the global 250kb parser at L115 (same pattern as the /api/vision line at L114). Without this the cloud audio path is dead. Add createApp.ts json-limit to filesToEdit notes.

Fix cloudbuild.prod.yaml: keys go via a NEW '--set-secrets' arg (e.g. '- SOAPBOX_API_KEY=arbor-soapbox-key:latest'), NOT inside --set-env-vars. Non-secret vars (CHILD_ASR_PROVIDER, SOAPBOX_API_URL/WHISPER_API_URL) append to the packed ^@^ string at L49. Pre-create the Secret Manager entries in arborprd-westeu and grant the Cloud Run SA roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor.

Rename consistency: use the existing api.grantConsent (lib/api.ts:204) in VoiceConsentGate, or add a setConsent alias; api.listConsent already exists (L206) so do NOT re-add it. Drop 'add listConsent' from the spec.

Trim already-done items from filesToEdit scope notes: speechScorer.ts already accepts+passes childId (L50,60-65); SpeechCoachTab already passes childProfile.id (L125) and already guards level!=='story' (L123); api.scoreUtterance already sends childId (L173). The remaining real work in these files is only the needsConsent handling + opening VoiceConsentGate.

Native manifests are genuinely missing the perms (Info.plist has no NSMicrophoneUsageDescription; AndroidManifest.xml:34 has only INTERNET) — these two edits are correct and real. Keep them. Add RECORD_AUDIO and (Android) consider MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS.

childAsr.realmap.test.ts is genuinely new (only src/server/childAsr.ts exists, no test) — correct. The integration test can reuse LocalConsentStore + buildConsentGrant (confirmed exports, consent.ts:103,135; requireConsent.test.ts:4 already imports them).

spec.title: MimicStudio MediaPipe cross-platform hardening
spec.goal: Make the MimicMatch / MimicStudio camera + MediaPipe FaceLandmarker experience work reliably on iOS and Android native (Capacitor) — not just web. Concretely: (1) declare and request camera permission on both native platforms so getUserMedia succeeds in the webview; (2) self-host the MediaPipe WASM runtime + face_landmarker.task model so they are same-origin, service-worker-cacheable, and survive CDN/CORS/offline failures on native; (3) add a perf throttle (detection cadence cap) so the per-frame detectForVideo loop does not melt low-end devices; (4) make degradation graceful and observable (clear unavailable/permission-denied states, telemetry) and finish the MimicStudioTab i18n so HE parents see translated mirror/rating copy. Privacy invariant is preserved: frames stay on-device, only the star rating persists.
filesToCreate: C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\src\lib\cameraPermission.ts — thin cross-platform camera-permission helper: on native dynamically import @capacitor/camera and call Camera.checkPermissions()/requestPermissions(); on web resolve to 'granted' (getUserMedia prompts itself). Returns 'granted'|'denied'|'prompt' so callers can branch. Dynamic import keeps the plugin out of the web bundle (mirror native.ts pattern).

C:\Users\dguyr\ROS\PPPPtherapy-\PPPPtherapy-\app\public\mediapipe\.gitkeep — directory marker; the vision WASM (vision_wasm_internal.{js,wasm}) and face_landmarker.task model are copied here at build time so they are served same-origin (cacheable by public/sw.js, bundled into dist/ and shipped inside the native apps for offline).

missingPieces: BLOCKER: the float16 face_landmarker.task model is NOT in node_modules/@mediapipe/tasks-vision. The script must instead download the model (curl/fetch from the Google Storage URL) at build time with a pinned SHA256 integrity check, or the model must be committed to public/mediapipe/ directly. spec.title: Growth Plan full auto-regenerate loop (assess → weekly → regenerate)
spec.goal: Close the growth-plan loop. Today a plan is one-shot: handleGenerateActionPlan always mints a brand-new plan (plan-${Date.now()}) and PlansTab tells the family to "start a fresh plan" when complete (PlansTab.tsx:146). M9 adds a regenerate-in-place path: feed the existing plan's completed-vs-missed steps, the child's new behaviorLogs since the plan was made, and the milestone/mission signal into /generate-plan so the AI carries forward the same challenge, drops what's done, intensifies what stalled, and addresses newly-logged patterns — persisting back to the SAME plan id (preserving history), not a new one. Builds on lib/plans.ts (planProgress/suggestedChallenges) and signals.ts (weeklyMissionPlan).

Plan-id timestamp parsing for the new-log window: ids are `plan-<ms>` (ArborContext.tsx:767) and actionPlans sorts on a /(\d{10,})/ regex (L177); seeded defaultActionPlans / older plans may not match — collectRegenInputs must fall back (e.g. last 21 days, matching suggestedChallenges windowDays) when no timestamp is parseable, or new logs get dropped silently.

Overwriting the plan in place loses the prior step set; if a parent regenerates by accident there is no undo. Mitigate by confirming, or store prior phases under a `history` field (optional, can be a follow-up).
250kb JSON body limit (createApp.ts:112): trim newLogs client-side (cap count + drop photos) before sending; behaviorLogs can carry newLogPhoto data.
i18n: shipping the regenerate button as hardcoded English would extend the existing Hebrew-facade debt — must add t() keys.

Three overlapping growth loops (plans/missions/bands) remain siloed; M9 only closes the plan loop. Optionally feed weeklyMissionPlan focus into the regen prompt, but keep scope tight to avoid coupling to signals.ts score concepts.
spec.deployNotes: "Backend + frontend change — requires a Cloud Run redeploy (cloudbuild.prod.yaml) because /generate-plan behavior (or the new route) lives server-side; frontend ships to Firebase Hosting (arborprd-westeu). Sequence backend-first so the client's regenerate call has an endpoint before the UI exposes the button. No new env vars. If the single-route approach is taken, no entitlement-config change is needed (inherits generate-plan's existing aiQuota + advancedPlans gate). Verify in prod: regenerate as a Plus user succeeds and as a free user returns 402; confirm ai.usage telemetry records the regen call (it routes through modelProvider like generate-plan)."

missingPieces: PlansTab.tsx must additionally destructure handleRegeneratePlan from useArbor() (step 8 implies it but it's not in the filesToEdit destructure note); the existing destructure block is L14-26.

collectRegenInputs log-window anchor precedence is under-specified: on 2nd+ regenerations anchoring on the plan-creation timestamp re-feeds already-processed logs. Define explicit order: use plan.lastRegeneratedAt when present, else parse `plan-<ms>` from id, else fall back to last 21 days (matching suggestedChallenges windowDays).

Body-trim risk names the wrong field: BehaviorLog photo field is `photoAttachment?: string` (types.ts:143), not `newLogPhoto`. The client-side trim before POST must drop `photoAttachment` to respect the 250kb limit.

Context value export: step 7 says export handleRegeneratePlan alongside isPlanGenerating (~L878+); the actual return object is at L878 with isPlanGenerating at L936 and handleGenerateActionPlan at L973 — the new handler must be added to that object explicitly.
conflicts: api.ts is the single hottest backend file (all AI routes). The single-route extension of the existing /generate-plan handler (api.ts:1161) is a focused, low-blast-radius edit and avoids touching createApp.ts guard arrays — any sibling mission also editing api.ts should coordinate, but M9's preferred path adds no new route so no guard-array clobber.
types.ts ActionPlan (L171-182) append-only field additions are backward-compatible; any sibling mission editing the same interface must merge field additions rather than reorder.
i18n.ts en (around L253) and he (around L816) plan.* blocks — sibling missions adding i18n keys touch the same two blocks; additive, low risk but a merge point.

lib/api.ts generatePlan wrapper (L136-137) — if the single-route path is taken, extend this payload type rather than add api.regeneratePlan; a sibling mission editing api.ts client wrappers shares this file.
safetyGaps: Cost/entitlement gate is correctly preserved ONLY on the single-route path (inherits createApp.ts:121 aiQuota + :128 requirePlusFeature 'advancedPlans'). If the implementer instead adds POST /api/regenerate-plan and forgets EITHER guard array, regeneration becomes a free unmetered AI call — the exact cost+paywall hole called out for image routes. The spec flags this but the build must default to the single-route path to eliminate the risk.

Silent-drop privacy/UX bug: defaultActionPlans seed id is 'plan-1' (initialData.ts:76) which does NOT match the /(\d{10,})/ window regex; without the 21-day fallback, ALL new logs are dropped and regen carries no new behavior signal. Must implement the fallback or the closed loop silently does nothing for seeded/legacy plans.

No-undo data loss: in-place upsert overwrites the prior step set with no history. A11y/UX gap — add a confirm() before regenerate, or persist prior phases under an optional `history` field (spec marks this optional; for a parent-facing destructive action a confirm is the minimum bar).

screenForImmediateEscalation must run on the regen path against challengeTopic AND the foldedu-in newLogs free-text (trigger/response/notes) — the spec only mentions guarding challengeTopic; newly-logged behavior text could contain escalation signals that bypass the 409 if only the topic is screened.
corrections: Take the single-route path (extend /api/generate-plan at api.ts:1161 with optional priorPlan/completedSteps/missedSteps/newLogs) — do NOT add /api/regenerate-plan. This inherits the aiQuota (createApp.ts:121) and advancedPlans gate (createApp.ts:128) with zero createApp.ts edits, closing the cost/entitlement hole by construction. Remove createApp.ts from filesToEdit/sharedFilesTouched on this path.

In collectRegenInputs, define explicit window-anchor precedence: plan.lastRegeneratedAt ?? parse(/(\d{10,})/ from id) ?? (today - 21 days). Cover all three in planRegen.test.ts including the 'plan-1' seed case that must hit the 21-day fallback.
Client trim before POST must drop `photoAttachment` (the real BehaviorLog field, types.ts:143) and cap newLog count to stay under the 250kb express.json limit (createApp.ts:115).
Screen newLogs free-text (trigger/response/notes) through screenForImmediateEscalation in addition to challengeTopic on the regen branch, so newly-logged escalation signals still 409.

Add handleRegeneratePlan to PlansTab's useArbor() destructure (L14-26) and to the context return object (ArborContext L878+). Mirror handleGenerateActionPlan's PaywallError→openPaywall('advancedPlans') exactly (L774).

PlansTab.tsx: replace L144-147 copy AND surface a secondary regenerate button for high-but-incomplete (stalled) progress; gate all new strings behind t() with new keys plan.regenerate/plan.regenerating/plan.regenDesc added to BOTH the en (~L255) and he (~L818) blocks.

specs.title: JITAI nudges as real notifications (Capacitor Local/Push) off logged state

spec.title: JITAI nudges as real native + web notifications timed off logged state
spec.goal: Turn the existing in-app-only JITAI engine (src/lib/jitai.ts nextNudge crossed with predictRhythm) into actually-delivered notifications: schedule ONE local notification at the rhythm-derived receptive hour via @capacitor/local-notifications on iOS/Android, and use the web Notification API on web, gated behind an explicit opt-in toggle with quiet-hours, snooze, and a tap deep-link into the relevant tab. Closes the open loop where predicted timing never times an actual send.
filesToCreate: PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/src/lib/nativeNudge.ts (Capacitor local-notifications wrapper: dynamic import, web no-op, permission request, schedule/cancel, channel registration helper)
PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/src/lib/jitaiStore.ts (localStorage opt-in prefs + quiet hours + snoozedUntil + lastScheduled dedupe key, keyed arbor.jitai.<childId>)
PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/src/lib/nativeNudge.test.ts (web no-op assertions, quiet-hours suppression, send-hour selection, dedupe-per-day)
PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/src/lib/jitaiStore.test.ts (defaults, quiet-hours window math incl. overnight wrap, snooze suppression, load/save round-trip)
filesToEdit: PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/src/lib/native.ts (append ONE new try/catch block inside initNativeShell to register the arbor-nudges Android channel and attach localNotificationActionPerformed listener -> set window.location.hash to nudge tab; never reorder status-bar/keyboard/splash blocks)

PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/src/components/overview/RemindersCard.tsx (add Smart Nudges opt-in toggle + quiet-hours time inputs; wire enable() to nativeNudge permission + jitaiStore prefs; gate the existing fixed-clock daily Notification so it does not double-fire when a JITAI nudge is scheduled today)

PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/src/components/tabs/OverviewTab.tsx (after nextNudge resolves a non-null nudge, call scheduleNudgeNotification with the rhythm send-hour when opt-in is on and not in quiet hours/snooze; pass childName; scope edits to the existing nudge <section> only)
PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/capacitor.config.ts (add plugins.LocalNotifications smallIcon/iconColor config block; do not touch existing SplashScreen/StatusBar blocks)

PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/ios/App/App/Info.plist (no usage-string needed for local notifications, but confirm UIBackgroundModes not required; add nothing unless cap sync requires — verify after sync)

PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml (add <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS"/> for Android 13+; add the plugin's SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM only if exact timing is required)
PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/package.json (add @capacitor/local-notifications ^8 to match the other @capacitor/* ^8 deps)

apiChanges: NONE — strictly on-device. No new backend route, no FCM/APNs token, no Cloud Run change. Local notifications only (COPPA-clean, no remote push server). This is the key scoping decision: delivery is local-notification scheduling, not server push.
dataModel: jitaiStore Prefs (localStorage, per child, NO PII / no behaviour content): { smartNudges: boolean (opt-in, default false); quietStart: string 'HH:MM' (default '21:00'); quietEnd: string 'HH:MM' (default '07:00'); snoozedUntil?: string ISO; lastScheduledKey?: string (kind+YYYY-MM-DD dedupe); permissionAsked?: boolean }
No new server/Firestore types. No changes to src/types.ts. Receptivity-learning model is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for M10 (defer to the broader p2-6 engine work).
dependencies: npm: @capacitor/local-notifications ^8 (peer-matches @capacitor/core ^8.4.0 already present)
Run `npx cap sync` after install so ios/ and android/ pick up the plugin (regenerates Pods + Gradle entries)
Existing: @capacitor/app ^8.1.0 (already present, used for hash-route deep-link is optional; setActiveTab/hash routing already exists via tabFromHash in ArborContext)

Soft dep on rhythm read being dependable: predictRhythm already wired in OverviewTab.tsx:55; engine returns null below confidence threshold so notifications stay silent until rhythm is usable (no extra work)
No dependency on p4/p2-5 for THIS notification-only slice (those are needed only for the receptivity-learning superset in p2-6)
steps: 1. Add @capacitor/local-notifications ^8 to package.json dependencies; npm install.

2. Create src/lib/jitaiStore.ts: load/save Prefs keyed arbor.jitai.<childId>, helpers isQuietHour(now, prefs) (handle overnight wrap quietStart>quietEnd), isSnoozed(now, prefs), snooze(prefs)=+3h, markScheduled(prefs, kind, dateKey). Mirror the localStorage try/catch pattern in RemindersCard.tsx:36-52.

3. Create src/lib/nativeNudge.ts: export scheduleNudgeNotification(nudge, sendHour, childName) and cancelNudgeNotifications(). Guard with isNativePlatform from runtime.ts; dynamic-import @capacitor/local-notifications (mirror native.ts:13-37 no-op-on-web pattern). Request permission lazily; if denied, no-op. Build a numeric id deterministic from kind+date so re-renders don't duplicate. Schedule at sendHour:00 local today (or tomorrow if past), extra:{ tab: nudge.action }, title=nudge.headline, body=first sentence of nudge.body truncated ~120 chars. Add registerNudgeChannel() for Android (id 'arbor-nudges', default importance) and a web fallback path using window.Notification when !isNativePlatform && permission granted.

4. Edit src/lib/native.ts: APPEND a new try/catch inside initNativeShell (after the splash block, do not reorder) that calls registerNudgeChannel() and attaches LocalNotifications.addListener('localNotificationActionPerformed', e => { const tab = e.notification.extra?.tab; if (tab) window.location.hash = `#/${tab}`; }) — the existing hashchange listener (ArborContext tabFromHash, App lines 416/418) routes it.

5. Edit OverviewTab.tsx: where nudge is computed (line 149), add an effect that, when nudge!=null AND jitaiStore prefs.smartNudges AND !isQuietHour AND !isSnoozed AND not already scheduled today, computes sendHour from rhythm (frictionPeak-1 / windDownHour / else now+1, clamped out of quiet hours) and calls scheduleNudgeNotification; then markScheduled. Wrap in try/catch — never crash Today. Scope strictly to the existing nudge <section>.

6. Edit RemindersCard.tsx: add a 'Smart nudges' toggle + two quiet-hours <input type=time> (reuse the existing toggle markup at lines 110-119). enable({smartNudges:true}) requests notification permission via nativeNudge (native) or Notification.requestPermission (web). Gate the existing fixed-clock daily Notification (lines 66-77) so it skips when prefs.smartNudges is on (avoid double-fire).
7. Edit capacitor.config.ts: add plugins.LocalNotifications { smallIcon, iconColor:'#34b277' } (additive, leave SplashScreen/StatusBar untouched).
8. Edit android AndroidManifest.xml: add <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS"/>.
9. Add i18n keys to LanguageContext.tsx (EN+HE, append-only): smart-nudge toggle label, quiet-hours labels, notification title/body fallbacks.
10. Add EN i18n + run npx cap sync; verify Info.plist needs no new key (local notifications need none on iOS).
11. Write jitaiStore.test.ts and nativeNudge.test.ts (web no-op, quiet-hours wrap, snooze, dedupe).
12. npm run lint (tsc) + npm test; then native verification per testPlan.
testPlan: Unit: jitaiStore.test.ts — defaults (smartNudges false), isQuietHour with overnight wrap (21:00-07:00 true at 23:00 and 03:00, false at 12:00), isSnoozed true while snoozedUntil future, load/save round-trip.

Unit: nativeNudge.test.ts — scheduleNudgeNotification is a no-op on web (Capacitor.isNativePlatform mocked false, no plugin import error), deterministic id per kind+date, sendHour clamps out of quiet hours, dedupe key prevents double-schedule same day.
tsc: npm run lint passes (all new optional fields keep existing nextNudge callers compiling).
Vitest: npm test green incl. existing jitai.test.ts unchanged.
Web manual: toggle Smart Nudges on -> browser permission prompt -> with seeded dependable-rhythm logs, a foreground Notification fires; quiet hours suppress it; toggle off cancels.

Android (npx cap run android, Android 13+ emulator): toggle on -> POST_NOTIFICATIONS runtime prompt -> notification scheduled at learned hour appears in tray even with app backgrounded; tapping it opens the correct tab (#/coach etc.).
iOS (npx cap run ios on a Mac/sim): toggle on -> iOS notification permission prompt -> local notification fires at scheduled time; tap deep-links to tab.
Regression: web build byte-for-byte unchanged when Smart Nudges off (default); RemindersCard daily nudge still works when smart nudges off and does NOT double-fire when on.
No-double-schedule: re-mounting Today twice in one day schedules at most one notification (dedupe key).
risks: native.ts is a HOT serial file (m1-ios-safe-area / surf-ios / surf-android also append): must land AFTER m1's safe-area block and ONLY append a new try/catch — never reorder status-bar/keyboard/splash. Coordinate ordering per CONFLICT-MAP.
OverviewTab.tsx touched by b1-daily-home / c1-rhythm / p2-5: confine edits to the nudge <section>; land after those Today reorgs settle to avoid clobber.

Android exact-timing: WorkManager/AlarmManager inexactness under Doze can delay the notification; for a daily nudge inexact is acceptable — avoid SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM (Play policy friction) unless precise timing is required.
Permission UX: requesting notification permission must be lazy (only after explicit opt-in) per the operating rules — do NOT prompt on launch.
iOS has never been built (needs a Mac); stale committed dist/ under ios/App/App/public means npx cap sync MUST run or an old frontend ships.
Double-fire risk between RemindersCard fixed-clock Notification and the JITAI native schedule — must gate as in step 6.
Bundle id still placeholder app.arbor.family — unrelated to M10 but blocks store delivery of the notification feature; flag to Guy.
Receptivity learning is intentionally descoped from M10; do not silently expand into the full p2-6 engine (separate larger mission).
spec.deployNotes: Frontend + native shells only — NO backend/Cloud Run deploy required (local notifications are on-device). Web: ships with the next hosting deploy of arborprd-westeu; works immediately (foreground Notification API), no env vars. Native: requires `npm i` then `npx cap sync` then rebuild the iOS (needs Mac) and Android apps and submit to stores — notifications will not reach existing TestFlight/Play builds without a new binary. No CORS/env change needed since no new API route. Verify Info.plist needs no new usage string after cap sync; confirm POST_NOTIFICATIONS is present in the merged AndroidManifest. Set a real bundle id before first store submission.

missingPieces: WRONG i18n target file. Spec routes all new EN+HE strings into src/context/LanguageContext.tsx, but that file contains NO string dictionaries — it only wires translate() imported from ../lib/i18n. The real dictionaries are in src/lib/i18n.ts (const en at line 14, const he at line 585). As written, the i18n edit lands in the wrong file and t('smartNudges...') returns the bare key. filesToEdit must list PPPPtherapy-/PPPPtherapy-/app/src/lib/i18n.ts, not LanguageContext.tsx.

TEST ENVIRONMENT MISMATCH (build-breaking). scripts/vitest.config.mjs sets environment:'node' with NO jsdom/happy-dom. There is no window, localStorage, or Notification in the test runtime, and NO existing *.test.ts touches them. jitaiStore.test.ts (localStorage load/save round-trip) and nativeNudge.test.ts (window.Notification web fallback) will throw ReferenceError unless each test file explicitly stubs globalThis.localStorage / globalThis.window / globalThis.Notification (e.g. vi.stubGlobal). The spec's testPlan does not specify this scaffolding. Either add per-test global stubs or switch the new tests to a jsdom environment via a // @vitest-environment comment.

Capacitor mock strategy for isNativePlatform is unspecified and the naive approach fails. runtime.ts evaluates `export const isNativePlatform = Capacitor.isNativePlatform()` at MODULE LOAD. nativeNudge.ts imports that const. Mocking Capacitor.isNativePlatform() AFTER import (as testPlan implies) is too late. The test must vi.mock('../lib/runtime', ...) or vi.mock('@capacitor/core') hoisted before import; the spec gives no mechanism, so 'no-op on web' test as described will not pass.

LOG-nudge deep-link is broken. jitai.ts nextNudge can return action:'log' (the LOG kind). Step 3 sets extra:{ tab: nudge.action } and Step 4's listener does window.location.hash = `#/${tab}`. But 'log' is NOT in VALID_TABS (ArborContext.tsx:99-101), so tabFromHash() returns null and the tap is a silent no-op — it does not open QuickLog (which is local React state setQuickLog(true) in OverviewTab, not hash-routable). Spec must special-case action==='log' (route to overview, or carry a query the OverviewTab reads to auto-open QuickLog).

No @capacitor/app deep-link wiring exists or is added. dependencies note claims hash routing 'already exists via tabFromHash' but there is currently NO App.addListener('appUrlOpen'|'resume') and no localNotificationActionPerformed handler anywhere in src/. On a cold tap the webview may not yet have the listener attached when the event fires; spec relies on a listener it is introducing in native.ts but never addresses the cold-start race or the App-resume case.

ios/App/App/public/ holds a committed stale frontend bundle (cordova.js, assets/, icon.svg present). cap sync regenerates it, but the spec marks Info.plist edit as 'verify after sync' and never commits to running the sync as a hard gate before any iOS test — the stale-dist risk is real for iOS even though it is NOT git-tracked.
conflicts: native.ts is a hot serial file. Spec itself flags m1-ios-safe-area / surf-ios / surf-android all append to initNativeShell. M10 must land AFTER m1's safe-area block and ONLY append a new try/catch — must not reorder the status-bar/keyboard/splash blocks. Sequencing dependency on m1 is real.

OverviewTab.tsx is touched by b1-daily-home / c1-rhythm / p2-5 (Today reorgs). M10 adds an effect near the nudge computation (line 143-155) and the nudge <section> (line 254-266). High clobber risk on the nudge block; M10 must land after those reorgs settle and confine edits to the existing nudge section.

src/lib/i18n.ts (the CORRECTED i18n target) is a shared append-only dictionary that many missions touch. Append-only with stable keys is required; any mission reordering en/he risks merge conflicts. capacitor.config.ts plugins block is also shared with any other native mission adding plugin config (additive merge required).
package.json dependencies block is shared with every mission adding a Capacitor plugin; additive insert only, but concurrent edits will conflict on the same hunk.
safetyGaps: COPPA/privacy: the local-only scoping is the right call and is COPPA-clean (no FCM/APNs token, no server push). BUT the notification title/body are built from nudge.headline/body which interpolate the child's first name (e.g. 'Get ahead of 5pm' / 'Around 5pm tends to be a harder stretch for Dylan'). A scheduled OS notification renders on the lock screen — child name + behavioural inference ('harder stretch') becomes visible to anyone who sees the locked device. Spec's dataModel says store 'NO PII / no behaviour content' but the delivered notification payload itself contains both. Needs a privacy-safe copy variant for the notification body (no name, no inferred-difficulty phrasing) distinct from the in-app nudge.

a11y: RemindersCard toggle pattern (role='switch', aria-checked, aria-label) is good and should be reused, but the two new quiet-hours <input type=time> need explicit aria-labels and the spec only says 'reuse markup' — must specify labels (and HE/RTL: time inputs + the toggle must mirror correctly; spec adds HE strings but does not call out RTL verification of the new row).
Cost: none (on-device, no API) — correctly scoped, no Cloud Run/token cost. Good.

Permission UX: lazy-on-opt-in is correctly specified (no launch prompt). One gap: Android 13+ POST_NOTIFICATIONS runtime request is triggered by the plugin only on first schedule/permission call; if the user toggles on but no dependable rhythm exists yet, no notification is scheduled and the permission prompt may never appear, leaving the user thinking it is 'on' but silent. Needs a clear UI state for 'on but waiting for enough data'.
corrections: Change filesToEdit: replace 'src/context/LanguageContext.tsx' with 'src/lib/i18n.ts' and append the new keys to BOTH const en (after ~line 570) and const he (after ~line 1121), keeping key parity. LanguageContext.tsx needs no edit.

Add test scaffolding to the spec: each new *.test.ts must either declare `// @vitest-environment jsdom` (and add jsdom/happy-dom to devDependencies + vitest config) OR explicitly vi.stubGlobal('localStorage', <map shim>), vi.stubGlobal('Notification', <stub>), and vi.mock('../runtime', () => ({ isNativePlatform: false, nativePlatform: 'web' })) hoisted before importing the unit under test. State this concretely in steps 11 and testPlan.

Fix the LOG deep-link: in nativeNudge scheduleNudgeNotification, when nudge.action==='log' set extra.tab to a hash-safe value (e.g. 'overview') plus extra.openQuickLog=true; OR map 'log' to 'overview' in the listener. Then OverviewTab must read that intent (e.g. a sessionStorage/hash query flag) to auto-open QuickLog. Without this, tapping a LOG notification dead-ends.

Add a privacy-safe notification copy path: do NOT pass nudge.headline/body verbatim into LocalNotifications.schedule. Use generic, name-free strings (e.g. title='Arbor', body='A good moment for a quick check-in') for the lock-screen payload; keep the rich, name-bearing copy for the in-app card only. Add these as separate i18n keys.

Make native.ts edit explicitly ORDER-dependent: append the new try/catch as the LAST block inside initNativeShell, after m1's safe-area block, and document in CONFLICT-MAP that M10 lands after m1/surf-ios/surf-android. Do not touch existing blocks.
Make `npx cap sync` a hard gate (not 'verify after') before ANY native build/test, given the stale ios/App/App/public bundle. Add it as an explicit blocking step, not a conditional.

Add an explicit UI state in RemindersCard for 'Smart nudges on, waiting for enough logged days' so the Android 13+ permission prompt timing and the silent-until-dependable behaviour are not mistaken for a bug.

Confirm the dedupe id is a stable 32-bit int: LocalNotifications requires a numeric id. 'numeric id deterministic from kind+date' must specify a hash that fits a signed 32-bit int (e.g. a small kind index * 100000 + dayOfYear), since arbitrary string-hashes can overflow and the plugin rejects/coerces large ids.

specs.title: AI cost governance: entitlement+quota gating for image/scene/adventure/ASR + telemetry

spec.title: AI cost governance: entitlement + quota gating for image/scene/adventure/ASR + telemetry
spec.goal: Close the cost hole where the highest unit-cost AI routes are completely unguarded. Today only the 8 text routes in createApp.ts L120-128 sit behind createAiQuota (80 req/hr) and createCoachMeter; the image trio (/generate-avatar, /generate-scene, /generate-comic), /voice, /score-utterance, /generate-adventure, /generate-hero-journey, and /live/token have NO per-user quota, NO entitlement gate, and NO per-feature cap — only the global 30 req/min/IP limit in createApp.ts L103-112. This mission: (1) put every expensive route behind a per-user, per-feature daily cap keyed in the existing UsageCounterStore; (2) gate image generation + child-ASR by plan (Free gets a small daily image allotment, Plus higher) via the existing entitlement seam; (3) make image-gen and ASR spend visible by recording a fixed-cost estimate into the existing usageRollup (image usageMetadata is usually null, ASR never records at all); (4) surface per-feature usage + remaining to the parent via the existing /api/entitlement payload + useEntitlement hook. Reuse createAiQuota/createCoachMeter/requirePlusFeature/UsageCounterStore/resolveEntitlement/recordUsageRollup patterns — do not invent a parallel system.

Mirrors aiQuota.ts + createCoachMeter shape exactly.

spec.deployNotes: "Backend + frontend change. Backend (Cloud Run via cloudbuild.prod.yaml, project arborprd-westeu, europe-west4) must deploy first — the new middleware + /entitlement fields are server-side. Set the cap env vars on the Cloud Run service (or accept the env.ts defaults). The aiQuota Firestore collection already exists with a TTL policy on expireAt; the new per-feature docs reuse it — no Firestore index or rules change needed (server uses admin SDK). Frontend (Firebase Hosting, https://arborprd-westeu.web.app) deploy follows: ships the widened EntitlementInfo type + PaywallModal/i18n copy. Native (iOS/Android Capacitor) require NO native rebuild for the gating itself — the webview calls the same /api via runtime.ts shim; a `npx cap sync` + redeploy is only needed if you want the updated paywall copy in the bundled dist (otherwise it loads from the prod web URL). Verify post-deploy: GET /api/entitlement returns the new usage fields; exceed an image cap to confirm 402; check /api/admin/overview shows image_gen/child_asr cost. Rollback: middleware is additive app.use blocks — comment them out and redeploy to disable enforcement instantly; no data migration to reverse."

specs.title: Unified COPPA consent granularity (face / voice / AI-training / sharing) + audit

spec.title: Unified COPPA consent granularity (face / voice / AI-training / sharing) + audit
spec.goal: Promote the existing M9 consent backbone into a complete, user-facing, fully-enforced, auditable consent system. Ship: (1) an append-only consent AUDIT trail (every grant/revoke/expiry preserved, not just the latest decision); (2) client API + a Privacy & Consent UI so parents can actually grant/review/revoke face_processing, voice_processing, ai_training, and share:* consents; (3) real wiring of every sensitive surface (AvatarCreator photo path, score-utterance/SpeechCoach, /voice spoken coach, MimicStudio camera acknowledgment, /shares share:* gate) to the server consent store so the client-only ephemeral checkbox is removed and the 451 consentRequired path becomes reachable and self-healing; (4) an enforcement seam for ai_training (currently a typed purpose nothing checks). Net: one consent model, persisted + audited records, each feature gated server-side, no client bypass.

Verify no caller sends a photo without also sending childId.

share:* gate on POST /shares is a behavior change: existing shares created without consent records will now 451. Mitigate with a one-time backfill or treat absence-of-record on legacy as a soft-allow-then-record migration — decide explicitly to avoid breaking current sharing users.
ai_training enforcement is a seam with no active call site today; risk of shipping dead code. Keep it minimal + tested, documented as the hook for future training pipelines.

MimicStudio frames never leave the device, so over-gating it (a hard server consent) would be incorrect privacy theater — keep it a transparency acknowledgment, not a server gate.

Firestore rules deploy is global; an incorrect rule could lock the server out (it uses Admin SDK which bypasses rules, so safe) but could also accidentally expose if written allow-true — copy the entitlements deny pattern exactly.
spec.deployNotes: backend+frontend (+ native). Backend-first: deploy the new /consent/audit route, audit store, share:* gate, and ai_training seam to Cloud Run via cloudbuild.prod.yaml BEFORE shipping the web client that calls them, so the client never 404s. Set CONSENT_POLICY_VERSION in Cloud Run env if the consent copy changed. Deploy firestore.rules with the consents/consentAudit deny rules (firebase deploy --only firestore:rules) alongside or just after the backend. Then build + deploy the web client (Firebase Hosting, arborprd-westeu, europe-west4). For native: after the web build, run npx cap sync to refresh ios/android public/, commit the Info.plist (NSCameraUsageDescription, NSMicrophoneUsageDescription) and AndroidManifest (CAMERA, RECORD_AUDIO) changes; iOS archive needs a Mac. Verify: (1) POST /consent then GET /consent/audit returns the event in prod; (2) photo avatar without consent returns 451 and the UI opens ConsentDrawer; (3) granting then generating succeeds; (4) /privacy/erase reports consents + consentAudit counts; (5) client cannot read consents/consentAudit directly (rules deny).

missingPieces: STALE PREMISE — AvatarCreator is already done. The spec's step 11 / filesToEdit for AvatarCreator.tsx ('remove the ephemeral local consent useState as the sole gate; call api.setConsent(face_processing) on tick; send childId') is ALREADY IMPLEMENTED in src/components/profile/AvatarCreator.tsx: it hydrates via api.listConsent(childId) (lines 76-88), calls api.grantConsent({purpose:'face_processing'}) / api.revokeConsent on toggle (lines 91-105), already passes childId into generateAvatar (line 64), and the header comment explicitly says 'not an ephemeral local checkbox'. The remaining real gap is only the 451->open-ConsentDrawer handoff. The spec must be rescoped or it ships a no-op/regression here.

STALE PREMISE — lib/api.ts client methods already exist. grantConsent/listConsent/revokeConsent are present (src/lib/api.ts lines 204-209); generateAvatar (151) and scoreUtterance (173) ALREADY include childId. ConsentGrant/ConsentPurpose client types already live in src/types.ts (lines 80-97, with active?:boolean). So the only genuinely NEW lib/api.ts additions are consentAudit(childId) + the ConsentAuditEvent type. The spec materially overstates this file's work and risks clobbering existing, correct code. (The spec's own name 'setConsent' also disagrees with the shipped name 'grantConsent' — pick one, don't add a duplicate.)

MISSING shared-file edits: types.ts is NOT in filesToEdit but MUST be edited — ConsentAuditEvent is a client type and the existing ConsentGrant/ConsentPurpose are defined there, not in lib/api.ts. The dataModel section even says 'No change to types.ts' which is wrong for the audit type.

MISSING the /voice consent-grant call site. gateVoiceSurface = requireConsent(consentStore,'voice_processing') is UNCONDITIONAL on POST /voice (api.ts line 563), and streamVoice() resolves childId via childProfile.id — so the spoken coach is ALREADY hard-gated and almost certainly 451-blocked in prod today because NO UI currently grants voice_processing consent (AvatarCreator only grants face_processing). The spec names '/voice spoken coach' in the goal but provides NO filesToEdit entry that grants voice_processing for the spoken-coach surface. Without it the consent system stays incomplete and /voice stays broken.

INCOMPLETE score-utterance wiring detail: the caller is lib/speechScorer.ts -> api.scoreUtterance (childId already plumbed from SpeechCoachTab.tsx childProfile.id line 125). The spec points at 'SpeechCoach or score-utterance caller' but the actual 451-handling/open-ConsentDrawer must be added in speechScorer.ts OR SpeechCoachTab.tsx, and NEITHER is in filesToEdit. There is also no UI that grants voice_processing before posting audio, so score-utterance is 451 today too.

MISSING decision on share scope identity. share:* gate uses the scope string as the suffix, but TrustedSharing.tsx sends human display labels with spaces/caps ('Story timeline','Therapist Summary'; SCOPE_OPTIONS line 11) while shares.ts examples use machine tokens ('timeline','reports'). The spec does not define the canonical scope vocabulary, so gateShare(`share:${scope}`) and the ConsentDrawer 'share scopes' rows will mismatch what TrustedSharing records. Must normalize scope tokens before this is build-ready.

AUDIT action type mismatch: consentAudit.ts type lists action 'granted'|'revoked'|'expired'|'reaffirmed'; the dataModel + risks add 'blocked' (denied-attempt logging in requireConsent). The union must include 'blocked' consistently across consentAudit.ts, lib/api.ts/types.ts, and requireConsent.ts or the requireConsent audit-on-451 feature won't typecheck.
conflicts: api.ts is the hottest backend file (every backend mission edits createApiRouter's destructure + ApiDeps). Adding consentAuditStore to ApiDeps (line 31-42) and the destructure (line 75) is additive but WILL textually collide with sibling missions that also append to the same two locations — must be rebased, not merged blind. The spec acknowledges this in risks; keep the injection strictly additive and append at the end of ApiDeps.

createApp.ts line 71/129: adding consentAuditStore alongside consentStore and into the createApiRouter deps call collides with any sibling mission injecting a new store the same way. Same additive-append discipline required.

firestore.rules: the spec says to mirror the entitlements 'allow read,write: if false' deny — but note the rules use a flat top-level match block; /consents/{id} and /consentAudit/{id} must be added as siblings of /entitlements (lines 66-72). No conflict with existing collection rules, but any concurrent rules-editing mission shares this single file.
lib/api.ts and types.ts are shared client surfaces touched by most client missions — high textual collision risk on the api object literal and the type exports.
i18n.ts (lib/i18n.ts) is a shared EN+HE string table edited by virtually every UI mission — adding the consent string block will collide; append a clearly-namespaced consent.* block.
safetyGaps: COPPA/privacy POSITIVE but under-specified: the spec correctly keeps MimicStudio as a transparency acknowledgment (frames never leave device — over-gating would be privacy theater) and ai_training default-false. Good. BUT it does not specify actorUid provenance for the audit trail when auth is sandboxed (actorOf falls back to 'local-sandbox', api.ts line 50) — audit events in non-prod will record 'local-sandbox' as the consenting adult, which undermines the legal value of the audit. Define behavior when uid is the sandbox identity.

Audit collection unbounded growth (flagged in risks but no mitigation): append-only consentAudit with no TTL is a cost + GDPR-minimization concern. eraseByChild covers per-child GDPR erase, but there is no retention/TTL policy for a child who is never erased. Flag a Firestore TTL policy as a follow-up at minimum.

Legacy-share migration is left as 'decide explicitly' — shipping the share:* gate WITHOUT a backfill decision will 451 every existing sharing user (TrustedSharing currently records NO consent before createShare, line 60). This is a live breaking-change to a shipped feature; the migration stance (soft-allow-then-record vs hard-gate) MUST be decided before build, not deferred.

a11y: ConsentDrawer a11y requirements (focus trap, Escape, return-focus, reduced-motion, RTL HE) are stated but there is no shared drawer/focus-trap primitive identified to reuse — the spec says 'reuse app drawer pattern' without naming it. Without a concrete shared primitive each new drawer reinvents focus management and regresses a11y. Identify the existing drawer component (ProfileEditDrawer is referenced — confirm it has a reusable focus-trap or this is net-new a11y work).

Native honesty gap is real and correctly flagged: Info.plist has NO NSCameraUsageDescription/NSMicrophoneUsageDescription and AndroidManifest has ONLY INTERNET (no CAMERA/RECORD_AUDIO). Verified. Without these the gated camera/voice surfaces cannot request perms on device — the consent UX is dishonest on native. These edits are mandatory, not optional.
corrections: Rescope the AvatarCreator edit to ONLY add 451->open-ConsentDrawer handling + i18n/a11y polish; DO NOT re-add a setConsent-on-tick path — it already exists (grantConsent on toggle). Verify you are not duplicating the existing useEffect hydrate (lines 76-88) or the toggleConsent handler (91-105).

Drop or downscope the lib/api.ts edit: only add consentAudit(childId) + ConsentAuditEvent. Do NOT add setConsent/listConsent/revokeConsent (shipped as grantConsent/listConsent/revokeConsent) or re-declare childId on generateAvatar/scoreUtterance (already present). Reuse the existing name grantConsent everywhere instead of introducing setConsent, OR add setConsent as an alias and update useConsent/ConsentDrawer to call it consistently.
Add types.ts to filesToEdit (add ConsentAuditEvent there, next to the existing ConsentGrant/ConsentPurpose at lines 80-97) and correct the dataModel line that says 'No change to types.ts'.

Add a concrete /voice voice_processing grant path: either (a) gate /voice with appliesWhen so it only requires consent when the parent opts into the spoken loop AND wire a grant in the voice-coach UI, or (b) add a one-time voice_processing grant prompt in the spoken-coach entry point. Name the actual component. Today /voice is unconditionally gated and no UI grants voice_processing, so the spoken coach is already blocked — treat fixing this as in-scope, not incidental.

Add lib/speechScorer.ts (or SpeechCoachTab.tsx) to filesToEdit for the 451->ConsentDrawer handling AND add a voice_processing grant before posting audio. The current spec entry 'SpeechCoach or score-utterance caller' is too vague; the real path is SpeechCoachTab.tsx line 125 -> lib/speechScorer.ts line 60 -> api.scoreUtterance.

Define the canonical share-scope token vocabulary and normalize TrustedSharing.tsx SCOPE_OPTIONS (currently display labels with spaces) to stable machine tokens before applying share:${scope} gating, so gateShare, the recorded consent, and the ConsentDrawer rows agree. Reconcile with shares.ts default ['timeline'].

Make the audit action union consistent: include 'blocked' in ConsentAuditEvent everywhere (consentAudit.ts, types.ts, requireConsent.ts) since requireConsent's denied-attempt logging needs it. Either commit to 'blocked' or drop the requireConsent audit feature.

Decide the legacy-share migration explicitly in the spec (recommend soft-allow-then-record on first post-deploy share for grants with no consent record) and add a test for it, otherwise the share:* gate is a breaking change to live users.
Specify actorUid handling for sandbox/unauthenticated audit writes (actorOf returns 'local-sandbox') so audit records are legally meaningful in prod and clearly marked in non-prod.

Confirm the reusable drawer/focus-trap primitive (inspect ProfileEditDrawer.tsx) and reference it by path in the ConsentDrawer spec; if none exists, add focus-trap as explicit scoped work rather than assuming reuse.

buildLevel 'backend-first' is correct (new /consent/audit route + audit store + share gate must deploy before the client calls them). Keep it. Effort 'L' is plausible but skewed: the backend audit store + api.ts wiring + firestore.rules is modest; the bulk is the ConsentDrawer + i18n + a11y + native perms + the (mis-scoped) client edits — net still L, but only after removing the already-done AvatarCreator/api.ts work.

This closes the gap where deployment-production.md L83 instructs `curl /api/health` against a route that does not exist.

steps: 1. VERIFY AVAILABILITY: confirm whether gemini-2.5-flash-image is served in europe-west4. Run `gcloud ai models list --region=europe-west4` / check the Vertex Gemini image-model region table; if absent, the supported value is typically `global` or `us-central1`. Record the finding in docs/image-model-availability.md.

2. env.ts: add `vertexImageLocation: process.env.VERTEX_IMAGE_LOCATION || process.env.VERTEX_LOCATION || process.env.GCP_REGION || 'europe-west4'` to ArborConfig and the loadConfig() object (next to vertexLocation L100). Add the field to the ArborConfig type with a doc comment.

5. Add health.ts router exporting createHealthRouter(config) returning a Router with GET '/health' (and mounted so /api/health also resolves) -> res.json of the config-derived fields above; never throws, never touches Firestore/model.

6. createApp.ts: import and `app.use(createHealthRouter(config))` immediately after requestContextMiddleware (L84) and BEFORE the rateLimit/auth chain (L103+) so uptime checks are unauthenticated and unmetered.
7. .env.example: add VERTEX_MODEL_IMAGE, VERTEX_IMAGE_LOCATION (with europe-west4 caveat), GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL under the Vertex block (after L22).

Rewrite docs/deployment-production.md: insert an image-model availability/verification section; replace the fictitious /api/health check with the now-real one; add an authenticated POST /api/generate-avatar smoke test; document VERTEX_IMAGE_LOCATION; add `gcloud run revisions list --service arbor-api --region europe-west4` to the rollback section so PREV_REVISION is discoverable.
9. From app/: `npm run typecheck && npm test` (env.ts + modelRouter touched -> existing tests must stay green).
10. Deploy backend first (gcloud builds submit --config cloudbuild.prod.yaml), curl /api/health, run the avatar smoke test, only then redeploy hosting if any client change shipped (none here).
testPlan: typecheck: `cd app && npm run typecheck` (env.ts ArborConfig field + modelRouter change).

unit: `npm test` — existing suite must stay green; modelRouter has no dedicated image test, so add a small test asserting getImageModel uses vertexImageLocation when set (mock @google-cloud/vertexai VertexAI ctor, assert location arg).
config test: assert loadConfig() defaults vertexImageLocation to vertexLocation when VERTEX_IMAGE_LOCATION unset, and overrides when set.
local smoke: start server with ARBOR_ENV=local, `curl localhost:3000/health` -> 200 JSON with vertexImageLocation/vertexModelImage echoed.

prod smoke (post-deploy): `curl https://arborprd-westeu.web.app/api/health` -> 200; signed-in `POST /api/generate-avatar` returns an image (proves gemini-2.5-flash-image resolves in the chosen region); an image route 4xx/5xx with a 'no image / blocked / NOT_FOUND model' error means the region is wrong -> flip _IMAGE_REGION and redeploy.
rollback drill: `gcloud run revisions list --service arbor-api --region europe-west4` then update-traffic to the prior revision; confirm /api/health still 200.
risks: gemini-2.5-flash-image may NOT be available in europe-west4 (Vertex image models are region-restricted, often global/us-central1 only). Today vertexLocation forces image gen into europe-west4, so prod avatar/scene/comic gen may already be 404-ing with model-not-found. This mission's separate VERTEX_IMAGE_LOCATION is the fix; getting the region wrong keeps image gen broken.
Cross-region image calls add latency and may incur egress; if image region != text region the Cloud Run SA needs aiplatform.user there too.
env.ts is a hot/shared file (M-class config touched by many missions) — adding a field is low-risk but rebase conflicts are likely; keep the diff to one field + one default line.
modelRouter.ts is hot (provider abstraction); the second memoized vertex client must not break the existing single-client text path or the multimodal fallback (modelForGeminiRequest).

createApp.ts is the #1 contended file (auth/quota/entitlement ordering). The health route MUST be mounted before createAuthMiddleware AND before rateLimit, or uptime checks will 401/429. Do not place it inside the /api auth chain.
The ^@^ delimited env string on cloudbuild L49 is fragile — a stray comma vs @ breaks CORS_ORIGINS parsing; append VERTEX_IMAGE_LOCATION with @, never a comma.
Adding /api/health unauthenticated is a (tiny) new unauthenticated surface — keep it config-echo only, no secrets, no Firestore, no model calls.
spec.deployNotes: "Backend-first. This is overwhelmingly a Cloud Run + runbook change; no React or native shell edits, so hosting redeploy is OPTIONAL (only needed if you also rebuild the client for unrelated reasons). Sequence: (1) verify image-model region, (2) set _IMAGE_REGION/VERTEX_IMAGE_LOCATION, (3) `gcloud builds submit --config cloudbuild.prod.yaml --substitutions=_REGION=europe-west4,_IMAGE_REGION=<verified> --project arborprd-westeu`, (4) curl https://arborprd-westeu.web.app/api/health, (5) authenticated /api/generate-avatar smoke test, (6) if broken, change _IMAGE_REGION and redeploy. Rollback: `gcloud run services update-traffic arbor-api --to-revisions <PREV>=100 --region europe-west4 --project arborprd-westeu` (find PREV via `gcloud run revisions list`). No client rollback needed. Env to set: VERTEX_IMAGE_LOCATION (via cloudbuild) — everything else already baked. project arborprd-westeu, region europe-west4."

missingPieces: testConfig.ts NOT in filesToEdit. Step 2 adds a REQUIRED field `vertexImageLocation: string` to the ArborConfig type, but app/src/testConfig.ts returns an explicitly-typed ArborConfig literal and does NOT set the field. This breaks `npm run typecheck` for the entire suite (every test using createTestConfig). The field must either be added to createTestConfig (e.g. vertexImageLocation: 'europe-west4') or made optional on the type.

Health route is mounted wrong for the stated smoke test. Step 6 mounts `app.use(createHealthRouter(config))` at app ROOT (after requestContextMiddleware), which exposes GET /health, NOT /api/health. But firebase.json only rewrites `/api/**` to Cloud Run (the `**` rewrite sends everything else to index.html). So the prod smoke `curl https://arborprd-westeu.web.app/api/health` (apiChanges + testPlan) will hit the SPA, not the API. The router must be mounted at `app.use('/api', createHealthRouter(config))` (and that yields /api/health, not /health). The spec's claim 'mounted so /api/health also resolves' is false for a root mount.

The actual mechanism to read the deployed commit/revision is unspecified. Spec wants {commit} in the health body but there is no COMMIT/GIT_SHA env wired in cloudbuild.prod.yaml (grep finds none). Cloud Run injects K_REVISION automatically (sibling spec-A/REL-4 use exactly that). M14 must either surface process.env.K_REVISION or add a COMMIT/_TAG-derived env to the cloudbuild env string; as written `commit` will always be undefined.

No concrete test mechanics for the proposed modelRouter test. getImageModel()/getVertex() are private and the VertexAI client comes from a dynamic `import('@google-cloud/vertexai')` inside getVertex(). No existing test instantiates VertexAI (multimodal.test.ts and modelRouter.test.ts only exercise pure helpers). Asserting the ctor `location` arg requires vi.mock of the dynamic import + reaching a private memoized promise — feasible but undemonstrated; 'add a small test' is hand-waving.

Cross-region IAM not actioned. risks/dependencies correctly note the Cloud Run runtime SA needs roles/aiplatform.user in the image region if it differs from europe-west4, but there is no step that grants it — only a note. If _IMAGE_REGION resolves to us-central1/global, image gen stays broken (403/permission) until that grant is made. Needs an explicit runbook step + gcloud command.

WebSearch/gcloud availability verification (step 1) is a precondition the spec defers to build time but never resolves. The chosen `_IMAGE_REGION` default is left as 'the value verified in step 1' — i.e. unresolved. Without the locked value the cloudbuild change is incomplete.
conflicts: DIRECT CLOBBER of REL-4 (spec-C) over the health router. The shared-file conflict map watch-list item #3 explicitly names REL-4 as the CANONICAL owner of createHealthRouter + the createApp mount, with OPS-2 and PERF-1 consuming it. REL-4 creates app/src/server/health.ts exporting createHealthRouter(config, {firestorePing, breaker}) with GET /api/healthz + GET /api/readyz, mounted `app.use('/api', createHealthRouter(...))` between createApp.ts L80 and L82. M14 independently creates a health.ts (at src/routes/health.ts) with a DIFFERENT signature createHealthRouter(config), DIFFERENT routes (/health + /api/health), and a DIFFERENT mount point. Two missions cannot both own the health router. M14 must instead EXTEND REL-4's createHealthRouter to add the vertexImageLocation/vertexModelImage config-echo fields, not create a parallel one.

createApp.ts is the #1 contended file (10 missions: OPS-2, REL-4, PERF-1, SEC-1, SEC-2, SEC-6, COST-1, REL-3, CMP-1/5/7, PERF-4). Per the conflict map, all createApp mounts must go through Track-1's single createApp owner as discrete coordinated mount lines. M14 adding its own mount line independently will collide; the health mount specifically is already spoken for by REL-4.

deployment-production.md rewrite overlaps OPS-2 (spec-A), which owns the deploy/rollback restructure of arbor-deploy.yml and creates docs/ops/deploy-and-rollback.md including the one-command rollback and PREV_REVISION discovery. M14 rewriting docs/deployment-production.md's rollback section duplicates/contradicts OPS-2's runbook. Consolidate: M14 should add only the image-model-availability verification + image smoke test, and reference (not re-author) OPS-2's rollback runbook.

cloudbuild.prod.yaml `^@^` env string (L49) is an append-only registry shared by ~15 missions (SEC-1/2/3/6, REL-2, PERF-4, COST-1/4, AI-1/2/6/8, OPS-2, CMP-7) and managed by a per-wave 'config batch owner' per the conflict map §D. (2) heroCard.ts EXISTS and is used by OverviewTab — M4 EDITS it. (3) EmotionAvatar.tsx EXISTS (1666 bytes) — M2/M6 references are valid. (5) lib/api.ts already has grantConsent/listConsent/revokeConsent and AvatarCreator already persists face_processing consent (L64-88) — M12's AvatarCreator/lib-api work is mostly redundant; only consentAudit + share gate + ConsentDrawer + types.ts ConsentAuditEvent remain. TEST-INFRA (repo-wide, blocks M1/M3/M4/M5/M6 component tests): vitest env='node', include='src/**/*.test.ts' — NO jsdom/happy-dom/@testing-library installed; .tsx tests won't even be collected, and document/window/localStorage/canvas/Image are all absent. ORDERING RATIONALE: foundations + frontend-only first (W1), backend cost/consent spine serialized (W2), AI features on the spine (W3), storage+native shells (W4), frontend identity features + the i18n/a11y sweep dead last (W5) because M13 must localize the FINAL string set after every other surface stops churning i18n.ts.
